Items.Title: React 与信号：10 年后 | React vs Signals: 10 Years Later
Items.Description:
旧的温斯顿丘吉尔语录如何？

那些不从历史中吸取教训的人注定要重蹈覆辙

尽管可能会添加更具讽刺意味的附录。

研究历史的人注定要袖手旁观，而其他人都在重复它。

在过去的几周里，我们看到了围绕细粒度反应性（被称为信号）在整个前端世界的复兴所带来的兴奋的高潮。

有关 JavaScript 中信号的历史，请查看我的文章：

事实上，信号从未消失。 它们作为第三方库或隐藏在框架的普通对象 API 后面默默无闻地存在了好几年。 尽管 React 和 Virtual DOM 出现的常见言论谴责这些模式不可预测且危险。 他们没有错。

很容易忘记，但关于信号的争论与我们 10 年前关于双向数据绑定与单向数据流的争论是一样的。 信号是可变状态！ 它们是具有新名称的绑定。 当更新流不可预测时，UI 作为状态函数的简单性就会丢失。

但这不仅仅是一场长达 10 年的辩论。 所以我想谈谈这些年来事情发生了怎样的变化，并提供SolidJS作为陪衬。

对某些人来说可能是这样。 但我也邀请考虑我们中的一些人之前已经经历过这种情况，甚至*开始*我们的职业生涯与设置/渲染分离。 对我们来说，React 是“修复”。

此次对话的核心是理解 React 是什么。 到目前为止，我对这个主题的解释之一来自 Dan Abramov 最早的一篇文章You're Missing the Point of React ，他在其中陈述 React 的真正优势是：

组合、单向数据流、免于 DSL、显式突变和静态心智模型。

甚至多年后，围绕 React 的思想领袖们提出了他们修复前端的想法。

但假设，如果他们不这样做呢？ 如果有其他方法可以解决当天的问题而不涉及如此激烈的重新调整，那会怎样？

可靠的选择

它甚至分享了组合、单向数据流和显式突变等理念，这些理念让 React 感觉像是 UI 开发的简单答案。 它的不同之处在于反应性世界之外的一切都是效果。 这几乎是 React 的对立面，React 将您所做的一切都视为纯粹的（就像没有副作用一样）。

当我想在没有任何模板的情况下使用 Signals 创建一个计数器按钮时，我会这样做：

 function MyCounter() { const [count, setCount] = createSignal(); const myButton = document.createElement("button"); myButton.onclick = () => setCount(count() + 1); // update the text initially and whenever count changes createEffect(() => { myButton.textContent = count(); }); return myButton; }

我会调用我的函数并返回一个按钮。 如果我需要另一个按钮，我会再做一次。 这是非常设置和忘记的。 我创建了一个 DOM 元素并设置了一些事件侦听器。 就像 DOM 本身一样，我不需要为我的按钮调用任何东西来更新。 它是独立的。 如果我想要一种更符合人体工学的写作方式，我会使用 JSX。

 function MyCounter() { const [count, setCount] = createSignal(); return <button onClick={() => setCount(count() + 1)}> {count()} </button> }

信号与过去不同。 他们的执行是无故障的。 它们是推/拉混合体，可以对预定的工作流程（如 Suspense 或并发渲染）进行建模。 并通过自动处理减轻泄漏的观察者模式。 多年来，他们不仅在更新方面而且在创作方面一直处于领先地位。

不变性

到目前为止，一切都很好？ 好吧，也许不是：

我发现很难相信 (a) 很容易添加仅 init 渲染逻辑和 (b) 使其响应式需要重构的方法，也可以组合。 这是我们*在* React 之前拥有的以及 React 解决的问题。

显然，这是 React 解决的问题。 他们具体解决了什么问题？

暂时搁置 Dan 的具体担忧，归结为不变性。 但不是以最直接的方式。 信号本身是不可变的。 您不能修改他们的内容并期望他们做出反应。

 const [list, setList] = createSignal([]); createEffect(() => console.log(JSON.stringify(list()))); list().push("Doesn't trigger"); setList(() => [...list(), "Does trigger"]);

即使对于使用.value的 Vue、Preact 或 Qwik 的变体，您也是在替换值而不是通过赋值改变它。 那么信号是“可变状态”是什么意思呢？

具有细粒度事件驱动架构的好处是可以进行隔离更新。 换句话说，变异。 相比之下，React 的纯渲染模型抽象出底层的可变世界，每次运行都重新创建其虚拟表示。

如果数据接口是显式的、副作用得到管理并且执行定义明确，那么在查看两个驱动状态更新的声明性库时，这种区别有多重要？

单向流

我不喜欢双向绑定。 单向流是一个非常好的东西。 我经历过这些推文中提到的相同事情。 您可能已经注意到 Solid 在其原语中采用了读/写隔离。 这甚至适用于其嵌套的反应式代理。

如果你创建一个反应原语，你会得到一个只读接口和一个写接口。 关于这一点的观点在 Solid 的设计中根深蒂固，以至于社区成员喜欢嘲笑我，滥用 getter 和 setter 来伪造可变性。

我想对 Solid 的设计做的重要事情之一是保持思维的局部性。 父母是否使用信号并不重要。 您根据需要创作。 如果需要，请将每个prop视为反应式，并在需要时访问它。 不需要全局思维。 无需担心可重构性。

我们通过建议在编写 props 时访问 Signal 的值而不是将其传递下去来强化这一点。 让您的组件期望值而不是信号。 如果 Solid 可能具有反应性，则通过将它们包装在 getter 中来保留反应性。

 <Greeting name={name()} /> // becomes Greeting({ get name() { return name() }) <Greeting name={"John"} /> // becomes Greeting({ name: "John" })

它怎么知道的？ 一个简单的启发式。 如果表达式包含函数调用或属性访问，它会将其包装起来。 因此，任何函数调用或属性访问（可能是 getter 或代理）都可能是反应式的，因此我们将其包装起来。

积极的是，无论您如何使用Greeting ，您都可以以相同的方式访问该属性： props.name 。 没有不必要的isSignal检查或过度包装来将事物变成信号。 作为一个价值，没有突变的期望。 道具是只读的，数据以一种方式流动。

选择加入与选择退出

哪种方式，框架作者

这可能是讨论的重点。 有很多方法可以解决这个问题。 出于开发人员体验的原因，大多数图书馆都选择了反应性，因为自动依赖性跟踪意味着无需担心丢失更新。

对于 React 开发人员来说，这并不难想象。 没有依赖数组的图片挂钩。 同样，您在使用 React 服务器组件时选择加入客户端组件（ use client ）。 多年来，还有其他解决方案通过编译自动执行此操作，但有时要明确说明一些事情。

这通常不是一个单一的决定。 在任何框架中，您都可以选择加入和退出。 实际上，所有的框架可能更像是这样的：

哪种方式，框架作者 https://t.co/ZWFlX1XaRI

框架的理想无可非议，但现实并非如此明确。

这让我想到了这个例子：

对于信号，您必须考虑如何传播每个单独的值。 例如，在 Solid 中，这些组件做不同的事情！ 考虑每个单独的道具是否可以改变需要全局推理而不是局部推理。

从 Solid 的角度来看，这是两个截然不同的函数，因为 Solid 的 JSX 处理以及它们只运行一次的事实。 一旦您意识到这一点，就不会模棱两可并且很容易避免。 甚至还有一个 lint 规则。

这就像期待这些是一样的：

 const value = Date.now(); function getTime1() { return value; } function getTime2() { return Date.now(); }

移动表达式不会改变Date.now()的作用，但提升会改变函数的行为。

也许它不太理想，但这种心智模式并非没有它自己的好处：

另外，您还可以获取 LSP 引用以进行更改。

只是看起来....最理性

这真的可以“修复”吗？

这是合乎逻辑的后续行动。 这在很大程度上是一个语言问题。 编译器面临的挑战是更难解释边缘情况并理解出现问题时会发生什么。 这在很大程度上是 React 或 Solid 在历史上一直非常谨慎地保持清晰界限的原因。

自从首次引入 Solid 以来，我们就有人探索不同的编译方式，因为作为基元的信号具有很强的适应性和高性能。

两个系统都有规定。 那是因为如果你这样做，你可以观察到抽象泄漏，如果它们曾经优化过引擎盖下的东西。 包括可能不会重新运行组件的某些部分。

最终结果几乎是一样的。

最后的想法

所有这一切中最奇怪的部分是 React 团队在查看 Reactivity 时并不觉得他们在照镜子。 通过添加 Hooks，他们牺牲了部分重新渲染的纯度，以获得接近 Signals 的模型。 通过添加一个编译器来移除与记忆有关的钩子，他们完成了这个故事。

现在应该明白了，这些都是独立开发的。 或者至少没有给出任何确认，考虑到老卫士的观点，这并不奇怪：

抛弃 React 编程模型并采用信号之类的东西（或者更糟，信号加 dsl）是一个巨大的倒退，大多数人还没有将其内化

幸运的是，今天的 React 也不是 10 年前的 React。

他们之所以这样做，是因为坚信自己是混乱海洋中独特的理性声音。 由于他们的出色工作，我们取得了今天的成就，无数人从他们的教训中吸取了教训。

我们喜欢信号的性能特性。 我们不喜欢您必须编写代码才能获得它。

时代变了。 被“固定”掉的范式重新出现几乎是合适的。 它关闭了循环。 完成故事。 当所有的噪音和部落主义都消失后，剩下的就是良性竞争推动网络向前发展的故事。

断章取义拼凑一个故事可以说是不礼貌的。 时代在前进，观点在改变。 但这是 React 的思想领导层长期以来强烈持有的观点。 他们从一开始就大声疾呼。 我能够毫不费力地从过去几天的对话中获取所有这些引述。

有关 React 早期历史的更多信息，请观看：



原文:

How does the old Winston Churchill quote go?

Those who fail to learn from history are doomed to repeat it

Although a more ironic addendum might add.

Those who study history are doomed to stand by while everyone else repeats it.

In the past few weeks, we've seen the culmination of a build of excitement around the revival of fine-grained reactivity, being referred to as Signals, across the front-end world.

For a history of Signals in JavaScript check out my article:

The truth is Signals never went away. They lived several years in obscurity as third-party libraries or hidden behind frameworks' plain object APIs. Even though the common rhetoric that came in with React and the Virtual DOM condemned the patterns as unpredictable and dangerous. And they weren't wrong.

Easy to forget, but the debate about signals is the same one we had about 2-way data binding vs unidirectional data flow 10 years ago. Signals are mutable state! They’re bindings with a new name. The simplicity of UI as a function of state is lost when updates flow unpredictably.

But there is more to it than a 10 year old debate. So I want to talk about how things have changed over the years and offer SolidJS as a foil.

"Fixing" Front-end

@thdxr there’s an assumption that people with React brain are unfamiliar with other approaches. that’s probably true for some. but i also invite to consider that some of us have been through this before and even *started* our careers with setup/render split. to us, React was the “fix”.

At the core of this conversation is understanding what React is. React is not its Virtual DOM. React is not JSX. To this date one of my explanations on the subject came from one of Dan Abramov's earliest articles You're Missing the Point of React, where he state React's true strengths are:

composition, unidirectional data flow, freedom from DSLs, explicit mutation, and static mental model.

React has a very powerful set of principles that guide it that are more important than any implementation detail. And even years later there is this notion from the thought leaders around React that they fixed front-end.

But hypothetically, what if they didn't? What if there were other ways to address the problems of the day that didn't involve such drastic re-alignment?

A Solid Alternative

The concept behind Solid is equally simple. It even shares ideas like composition, unidirectional data flow, and explicit mutation that made React feel like a simple answer to UI development. Where it differs is outside the world of reactivity everything is an Effect. It's almost the antithesis of React which treats everything you do as being pure (as in having no side effects).

When I think of creating a Counter button with Signals without any templating I'd do this:

function MyCounter() { const [count, setCount] = createSignal(); const myButton = document.createElement("button"); myButton.onclick = () => setCount(count() + 1); // update the text initially and whenever count changes createEffect(() => { myButton.textContent = count(); }); return myButton;
}

I'd call my function and get a button back. If I need another button I'd do it again. This is very much set and forget. I created a DOM element and set up some event listeners. Like the DOM itself, I don't need to call anything for my button to update. It is independent. If I want a more ergonomic way of writing I use JSX.

function MyCounter() { const [count, setCount] = createSignal(); return <button onClick={() => setCount(count() + 1)}> {count()} </button>
}

Signals are not the same Signals as yesteryear. Their execution is glitch-free. They are push/pull hybrids that can model scheduled workflows like Suspense or Concurrent Rendering. And mitigate the leaky observer pattern with automated disposal. They have been leading benchmarks for several years not only for updates but for creation.

Immutability

So far so good? Well maybe not:

@thdxr it’s one of those “does it scale” things. i am finding it difficult to believe that the approach where (a) it’s easy to add init-only rendering logic and (b) making it reactive requires refactorings, composes just as well. it’s what we had *before* React and what React solved.

Apparently, this is something React solves. What did they solve exactly?

Putting Dan's specific concerns aside for a moment, it comes down to immutability. But not in the most direct way. Signals themselves are immutable. You can't modify their content and expect them to react.

const [list, setList] = createSignal([]); createEffect(() => console.log(JSON.stringify(list()))); list().push("Doesn't trigger"); setList(() => [...list(), "Does trigger"]);

Even with variants from Vue, Preact, or Qwik that use .value you are replacing the value not mutating it by assignment. So what does it mean that Signals are "mutable state"?

The benefit of having a granular event-driven architecture is to do isolated updates. In other words, mutate. In contrast, React's pure render model abstracts away the underlying mutable world re-creating its virtual representation with each run.

How important is this distinction when looking at two declarative libraries that drive updates off state if the data interfaces are explicit, side effects managed, and the execution well-defined?

Unidirectional Flow

I am not a fan of 2-way binding. Unidirectional Flow is a really good thing. I lived through the same things that are referenced in these tweets. You may have noticed Solid employs read/write segregation in its primitives. This is even true of its nested reactive proxies.

If you create a reactive primitive you get a read-only interface and a write interface. The opinion on this is so ingrained into Solid's design that members of the community like to troll me, abusing getters and setters to fake mutability.

One of the important things I wanted to do with Solid's design was to keep the locality of thinking. All the work in Solid is done where the effects are which is where we insert into the DOM. It doesn't matter if the parent uses a Signal or not. You author to your need. Treat every prop as reactive if you need it to be and access it where you need it. There is no global thinking necessary. No concerns with refactorability.

We re-enforce this by recommending when writing props you access the Signal's value rather than pass it down. Have your components expect values rather than Signals. Solid preserves reactivity by wrapping these in getters if it could be reactive.

<Greeting name={name()} />

// becomes
Greeting({ get name() { return name() }) <Greeting name={"John"} />

// becomes
Greeting({ name: "John" })

How does it know? A simple heuristic. If the expression contains a function call or property access, it wraps it. Reactive values in JavaScript have to be function calls so that we can track the reads. So any function call or property access, which could be a getter or proxy, could be reactive so we wrap it.

The positive is that for Greeting regardless of how you are consumed you access the property the same way: props.name. There is no isSignal check or overwrapping unnecessarily to make things into Signals. props.name is always a string. And being a value there is no expectation of mutation. Props are read-only and data flows one way.

Opt-In vs Opt-Out

which way, framework author

This might be the crux of the discussion. There are a lot of ways to approach this. Most libraries have chosen reactivity for Developer Experience reasons because automatic dependency tracking means not needing to worry about missing updates.

It isn't hard to imagine for a React developer. Picture Hooks without the dependency arrays. The existence of the dependency arrays in Hooks suggests React can miss updates. Similarly, you opt into client components (use client) when using React Server Components. There are other solutions that have been automating this via compilation for years, but at times there is something to be said about being explicit.

It isn't generally a singular decision. You have things you opt into and things you opt out of in any framework. In reality, all frameworks are probably more like this:

which way, framework author https://t.co/ZWFlX1XaRI

A frameworks ideals can be beyond reproach but the reality is not so clear cut.

This brings me to this example:

With signals, you have to think about how each individual value is propagated. For example, in Solid, these components do different things! Thinking about whether each individual prop can change or not requires global reasoning rather than local.

These are 2 very different functions from Solid's perspective, because of Solid's JSX handling and the fact they only run once. This is not ambiguous and easily avoided once you are aware. And there is even a lint rule for that.

It's like expecting these to be the same:

const value = Date.now();
function getTime1() { return value;
} function getTime2() { return Date.now();
}

Moving the expression doesn't change what Date.now() does but hoisting changes the function's behavior.

Maybe it is less than ideal, but it isn't like this mental model isn't without its own benefits:

@acdlite its funny, the first time i ever used signals wasn't for perf, it was for understanding where change happens easier. plus you get LSP references to change.

just seems.... most rational

Can this be "Fixed" for real?

That's the logical follow-up. This is very much a language problem. What does fixed even look like? The challenge with compilers is that it is harder to account for edge cases and understand what happens when things go wrong. It is largely the reason historically React or Solid has been pretty careful about keeping clear boundaries.

Since the first introduction of Solid we've had people exploring different compilation because Signals as primitives are very adaptable and very performant.

In 2021, I took a stab at it.

React team also announced they were looking at this too.

There are rules being imposed on both systems. React wants you to remember not to do impure things in your function bodies. That is because if you do you can observe abstraction leaks if they were ever to optimize what is under the hood. Including potentially not re-running parts of your component.

Solid is already optimized without the need for the compiler or extra wrappers like React.memo, useCallback, useRef, but like React could benefit from some more streamlined ergonomics like not having to worry about indicating where Signals are read.

The end result is almost the same.

Final Thoughts

The oddest part of all of this is that the React team when looking at Reactivity doesn't feel like they are looking in the mirror. By adding Hooks they sacrificed part of their re-render purity for a model approaching that of Signals. And that by adding a compiler to remove the hooks concerned with memoization they complete that story.

Now it should be understood these were developed independently. Or atleast no acknowledgement was ever given which is not surprising given the sentiment of the old guard:

@acdlite I think you guys should be way more aggressive in pushing back against this stuff. Throwing out the react programming model and adopting something like signals (or worse, signals plus a dsl) is a huge step backwards and most people haven’t internalized that

Fortunately React today is not the React of 10 years ago either.

React changed the front-end world by teaching us the important principles that should guide how to build UIs. They did so by strength of conviction being a unique voice of reason in a sea of chaos. We are where we are today due to their great work and countless have learned from their lessons.

@youyuxi @trueadm When @Huxpro introduced Forget at React Conf we focused on the auto-memoizing part, but the plan all along has involved compiling to a lower level reactive runtime. We like the perf characteristics of signals. We don't like the code you have to write to get it.

Times have changed. It is almost fitting that the paradigm that was "fixed" away would resurface. It closes the loop. Completes the story. And when all the noise and tribalism fade what remains is a tale of healthy competition driving the web forward.

It is arguably never polite to construct a narrative by sewing together quotes out of context. Time moves on, and perspectives change. But this is a long and strongly held sentiment from React's thought leadership. One they've been vocal about since the beginning. I was able to grab all these quotes over conversations from just the last couple of days with very little effort.

Items.Title: 新技术的缺点来得又快又猛（让我们谈谈人工智能） | The Downsides of New Technology Come Fast and Furious (Let's talk about AI)
Items.Description:
这篇文章是关于 AI 的危险，虽然围绕这个主题有很多“恐慌”和庆祝活动，但重要的是从那些正在整合或计划整合这些技术的人那里获得更多观点。 许多此类对话来自记者。 作为建设者，我们有一个需要检查的相关观点。

我们正处于计算的转折点。 生成式人工智能并非“无所不能”，但它是未来信息和真相的强大力量。 这些工具在现代社会中既被使用又被滥用，这与 OpenAI、Microsoft AI、Meta 的 LLaMa 以及该领域的所有新事物有关。 随着 OpenAI 的主要 API 的大幅降价，明天的实用程序将比今天的更强大。

虽然您不应该为对这项技术的兴奋感到羞愧，但必须注意的是，缺点不会明天出现； 他们今天来了，通常是在有利因素到来之前。 工具、基础设施和政府政策对于确保安全、公平和公平地使用我们最强大的技术是必要的，但最好的部分往往不会及时出现，或者永远不会出现。

我不想天真地把这些技术等同起来，但我认为值得一提的是：我们仍在等待加密的承诺，我们已经看到了显着的缺点。 拉高出货计划、犯罪圈子、环境破坏——到目前为止，不利因素比有利因素要明显得多。

我对 AI 感到兴奋，但随着它的好处来得快，我们也有可能更快地受到不利影响。 选择你的毒药：虚假信息、劳动力流失、欺诈等。让我们从混乱的角度来谈谈它。

混乱

制造混乱比防止混乱更容易，因为在短期内它通常需要更少的资源和更少的努力。 混乱可能由错误信息、缺乏沟通或计划不足引起，而这些因素可能更容易培养而不是解决。 防止混乱需要更多的资源、计划和协调。 它涉及识别潜在问题并采取积极措施在它们失控之前缓解它们。 这可能具有挑战性，因为它通常需要对根本问题有深刻的理解，并有能力采取果断行动来解决这些问题。

此外，混乱会产生滚雪球效应，小问题迅速升级为大问题，使局势越来越难以控制。 相比之下，防止混乱需要长期的持续努力，这可能很难维持。

总的来说，防止混乱在短期内需要更积极主动的努力和资源，但从长远来看，它可以帮助避免更大的成本和负面后果。

那么对于这一切该怎么办呢？

这篇文章不仅仅是一个模糊的警告。 让我们谈谈要推进的几个主题。

审查

让我们对创新感到兴奋，但也让我们始终如一地仔细检查这些版本。 在推出这些技术时，我们会看到不平衡的关怀和体贴。 我们需要赞美好的东西，仔细检查坏的东西。 要做出促进审查的具体决定，请寻找开源工具。 从长远来看，这通常是对您的发展最好的事情，但在其他条件相同的情况下，它有利于保障和安全。

安全保障

万维网最初并不是为了安全而构建的，网络安全行业在早期几乎不存在。 只有经过几十年的审查和关注，现代安全才得以正常化。 网络的采用以及社会对它的信任意愿，是一个足以让安全赶上的缓慢过程，也许还不够。 我们有 OWASP 前十名，我们有专门的会议。

人工智能时代的安全与保障将呈现出不同的形式，但它需要一个类似于其他网络安全领域的产业综合体。 对各类公司和政府的缓解投资需要规范化和优先化。

人工智能中的安全和安保结果本质上将不如我们习惯的那样具有确定性，消除混乱是一个艰难的攀登，但这是一个必须从今天开始的攀登。

感谢阅读，祝编码愉快！



原文:

This post is about the dangers of AI, and while there is plenty of "fear mongering" and celebration surrounding this subject, it's important to gain more perspective from those integrating or planning to integrate these technologies. A lot of this type of dialog comes from journalists. As builders, we have a relevant perspective that needs to be checked in on.

We are at an inflection point in computing. Generative AI is not "all-powerful", but it is a powerful force in the future of information and truth. These tools are both used and abused in modern society, and this is related to OpenAI, Microsoft AI, Meta's LLaMa, and everything new in this space. With OpenAI's primary API's significant price reduction, tomorrow's utilities will be more powerful than today's.

While you shouldn't feel ashamed of your excitement for this technology, it's essential to note that the downsides don't come tomorrow; they come today, often before the upsides arrive. Tools, infrastructure, and government policy are necessary to ensure the safe, fair, and equitable use of our most powerful technologies, but the best parts often don't surface in time, or ever.

I don't want to naively equate these technologies, but I think it's worth mentioning for illustration: We are still waiting on the promise of crypto, and we have seen a remarkable amount of downside. Pump and dump schemes, crime circles, environmental devastation — downsides so far have been so much more tangible than the upsides.

I am excited about AI, but as fast as the upsides come, we risk the downsides hitting us sooner. Pick your poison: Disinformation, workforce displacement, fraud, etc. Let's talk about it in terms of chaos.

Chaos

Creating chaos can be easier than preventing it because it typically requires fewer resources and less effort in the short term. Chaos can arise from misinformation, lack of communication, or insufficient planning, and these factors can be easier to cultivate than to address. Preventing chaos requires more resources, planning, and coordination. It involves identifying potential problems and taking proactive steps to mitigate them before they spiral out of control. This can be challenging because it often requires a deep understanding of the underlying issues and the ability to take decisive action to address them.

Moreover, chaos can have a snowball effect, where small issues escalate quickly into larger ones, making it increasingly difficult to control the situation. In contrast, preventing chaos requires a sustained effort over time, which can be challenging to maintain.

Overall, preventing chaos requires more proactive effort and resources in the short term, but it can help avoid much greater costs and negative consequences in the long term.

So what to do about all of this?

This post isn't just a nebulous warning. Let's talk about a couple of themes to move forward with.

Scrutiny

Let's be excited about the innovations, but let's also scrutinize these releases consistently. We will see uneven care and thoughtfulness in the rolling out of these technologies. We need to laud the good stuff and scrutinize the bad. For a tangible decision you can make to promote scrutiny, seek out the open source tool. This is often the best thing for your development in the long run, but all else being equal, it is good for security and safety.

Safety and security

The world wide web was not initially built to be secure, and the web security industry was virtually non-existent early on. Only after decades of scrutiny and focus did modern security normalize. Adoption of the web, and society's willingness to place trust in it, was a slow enough process for security to catch up, perhaps still not enough. We have an OWASP top ten, and we have dedicated conferences.

Safety and security in the era of AI will take a different form, but it needs an industrial complex akin to other cybersecurity fields. Investment in mitigation in every type of corporation and government needs to be normalized and prioritized.

Safety and security outcomes in AI are going to be inherently less deterministic than what we are used to, and allaying chaos is an uphill climb, but it is a climb that has to start today.

Thanks for reading, happy coding!

Items.Title: 在 JavaScript 中使用信号 | Making the Case for Signals in JavaScript
Items.Description:
在最近几周围绕 Signals 的所有讨论中，我忘记谈论可以说是最重要的话题。 你为什么要关心？ 我已经介绍了它们的演变和对立，但我还没有真正说明使用它们的理由。

围绕他们进行了与表演相关的叙述，这并非没有道理，但远不止于此。 这不仅仅是关于开发人员的经验。 它是关于颠覆当前的范式。

查看= fn（状态）

这是一个非常强大的思考 UI 的心智模型。 但更多的是它代表了一种理想。 要争取的东西。

现实要混乱得多。 底层 DOM 是持久的和可变的。 单纯的重新渲染不仅成本高得令人望而却步，而且还会从根本上破坏体验。 有一些方法可以减轻这种情况。 我们建立更好的结构，希望重塑我们的现实以适应。 但在某些时候，我们需要将实现与理想分开，才能坦诚地谈论这些事情。

因此，今天我们将按原样查看 Signals 以及它们必须提供的功能。

将性能与代码组织分离

这是你第一次意识到真正不同的事情正在发生的时刻。 不过，还有更多。 这不是要让你的应用程序充满全局状态，而是一种说明状态独立于组件的方式。

 function Counter() { console.log("I log once"); const [count, setCount] = createSignal(0); setInterval(() => setCount(count() + 1), 1000); return <div>{count()}</div> }

同样，当计数器更新时不重新执行的console.log是一个可爱的技巧，但并不能说明全部情况。

事实上，这种行为贯穿整个组件树。 源自父组件并在子组件中使用的状态不会导致父组件或子组件重新运行。 只有依赖它的 DOM 部分。 并且不仅仅是关于跨组件传播状态变化的影响，还有同一组件内的多个状态。

 function MoreRealisticComponent(props) { const [selected, setSelected] = createSignal(null); return ( <div> <p>Selected {selected() ? selected().name : 'nothing'}</p> <ul> {props.items.map(item => <li> <button onClick={() => setSelected(item)}> {item.name} </button> </li> )} </ul> </div> ); }

将示例从 Svelte Creator Rich Harris 的虚拟 DOM 移植到 SolidJS 是 Pure Overhead重要的是要理解，使用 Signals，更新选定状态除了更改<p>中的文本外不会导致任何执行。 没有重新运行列表或比较它。 如果其中一行中的名称更新，情况也是如此。 使用 Signals，我们可以直接更新那个按钮的文本。

注意：在 Solid 中，通常在渲染循环时使用<For>组件而不是.map ，以便在插入、删除或移动items的条目时不会重新创建每一行。

你可能会想，“我明白了。它很快。但我从来没有遇到过确保像 React 这样的良好性能的问题。” 但收获远不止于此。

您不再需要关注组件以实现最佳执行。

您可以将整个应用程序放在一个组件或多个组件中，并获得相同的好处。 您可以为自己的利益拆分组件。 你想如何组织你的应用程序。 不是因为您需要隔离发生变化的 UI 的某些部分。 如果性能曾经成为一个问题，那不会是因为组件的结构需要昂贵的重构。

这对开发人员体验来说并不是微不足道的好处。

将动态与静态分开

我的沮丧部分源于——是的，有很多事情让 React 变得混乱——但这是 React 有史以来最明显的胜利，如果它有的话。 消除第一个和下一个渲染之间的区别推动了它。 很难过看到这个被遗忘

有一些对话表明这是一件坏事。 如果您想了解更多观点，请参阅@dan_abramov对我之前文章的回复。

我不仅想谈论为什么这是一件好事，而且它实际上是一件了不起的事情。 能够针对每个进行优化是有益的。 这是与底层平台保持一致并带来红利的地方之一。

传统上讲，使用像 Signals 这样的分布式事件系统与自上而下运行的系统之间存在权衡。 虽然事件系统的更新速度会更快，但在创建时它有设置订阅的额外开销。

网络通常是面向文档的界面这一事实甚至使情况更加复杂。 即使在单页应用程序中，您也会进行大量涉及大量创建的导航。

说得通。 然而，Web 平台意识到了这种成本，并使得批量创建元素比单独创建元素更有效。 提取静态部分以进行大规模创建比那些订阅产生更大的影响。

好处并不止于浏览器。 对于基于信号的系统，代码的复杂性、大小和执行会随着 UI 的交互性而不是其中的元素数量而变化。

考虑一个交互很少的服务器呈现页面。 也许是电子商务网站。 静态部分是服务器呈现的 HTML。 您甚至不需要代码来使其具有交互性。 只需从包中删除静态部分。

来自 Builder.io 的 Steve（在 1:16）解释了它在Qwik中的工作原理：

诚然，这主要是性能问题。 它来自Islands architecture和React Server Components的相同动机。 它解决了我们今天面临的一个非常现实的痛点，即越来越大的 JavaScript 包和缓慢的初始页面加载趋势。

总的来说，我的立场是这种分离会带来一定程度的透明度。 它使解释和推理实际发生的事情变得更加容易。 虽然没有理想中那么简单，但它使逃生舱口（任何系统的重要组成部分）更加连贯。

通用化 UI 语言

我不是指编译语言。 信号完全是一种运行时机制。 这里没有魔法。 只是一个有向无环图。

虽然状态、派生状态和效果的概念似乎很清楚，但并非所有心智模型和实现都一致。

信号独立于任何组件或渲染系统。 并且只代表状态关系。 不像 React Hooks 这样有额外的原语来描述如何保护执行，比如useCallback 、 React.memo和像稳定引用（ useRef ）这样的概念来处理效果的协调。

文章底部列出的两篇 Dan 的文章都很好地探索了如何在 React 中有效地使用这些原语。

此外，信号有助于追溯。 它们为您提供了一种了解更新内容和原因的方法。

他们鼓励产生更多声明性代码的模式。 通过围绕数据而不是组件流组织代码，我们可以看到哪些数据正在推动变化。 它提出了一个关于代码用途的有趣问题。 我们应该优化它以使其更易于编写或更易于阅读吗？

好的，但是权衡呢？

肯定有权衡取舍。 最明显的一个是他们使数据变得特殊，而不是数据的应用。 我们不再处理普通对象，而是处理原始对象。 这与 Promises 或 Event Emitters 非常相似。 您正在推理数据流而不是控制流。

公平地说，在没有工具或编译帮助的情况下，任何 JavaScript UI 库或框架都是如此。 对于信号，这一点更加强调，因为您访问值的位置很重要。

我称此为 Signal 的（单数）Hook 规则。 这是有后果的。 有一个学习曲线。 它促使您以某种方式编写代码。 当使用代理之类的东西时，还有一些额外的注意事项，比如 JavaScript 语言中的某些机制（如传播、解构）限制了使用。

另一个考虑是围绕处置。 订阅链接是双向的，所以如果一侧是长期存在的，则可以比预期更长时间地保留内存。 现代框架非常擅长自动处理这种处理，但这是 Signal 设计所固有的。

最后，历史上存在对大型不可控图的担忧。 循环和不可预测的传播。 由于过去几年所做的工作，这些担忧在很大程度上已成为过去。 至于这些问题是 Signals 解决的问题，以及为什么你会在其他消息/事件系统上使用它们。

结论

实际上，许多工程师只是通过信号心智模型提高了工作效率——当发生这种情况时，在哲学层面上争论毫无意义。

有很多方法可以应对创建出色用户界面的挑战。 我的目标是让这个讨论保持基础，但我认为这里有很多值得兴奋的地方。

当您在基元基础上进行构建时，您可以做很多事情。 探索减少 JavaScript 加载开销和增加交互性是 Signals 自然适合的领域。

重要的是使用信号可以带来很大的好处，您不需要编译器。 甚至不用于模板。 您可以使用 Tagged Template Literals 并且无需构建步骤即可完成所有操作。 我们倾向于使用编译来使人体工程学更流畅。 但信号也是编译的绝佳选择。

当您拥有可以定位的高效构建块时，编译器和语言探索就会变得容易得多。 这不仅适用于我们，也适用于 AI。 我们已经看到这建议改进一切，从使用分析来驱动代码拆分以优化初始加载到优化编译器理解代码意图的能力。

对这条推文的回应有趣的是，人们对信号很感兴趣，而且我选择使用 React 的 API。

这是编译器。 想象一个 UI 编译器利用 AI 来自我改进…… twitter.com/trueadm /status……

我在业余时间一直在研究一个新的类似 React 的框架。 它完全由编译器驱动，没有虚拟 DOM 或组件重新渲染。 有信号，但它们从未暴露给开发人员，并且它们也被完全编译以避免任何运行时查找。 和…

无论信号最适合由开发人员持有还是作为机器的低级原语，它们似乎都是不断发展的 Web 前端世界中的重要一步。

组件是纯开销

在你之前 memo()作者：Dan Abramov



原文:

Amidst all the conversation around Signals the recent weeks, I have forgotten to talk about arguably the most important topic. Why should you care? I've covered their evolution and opposition, but I haven't actually made the case for using them.

There has been a narrative around them related to performance which is not without merit but it is so much more than that. It is more than about developer experience too. It is about flipping the current paradigm on its head.

React famously popularized:

view = fn(state)

Which is a very powerful mental model for thinking about UIs. But more so it represents an ideal. Something to strive for.

Reality is a lot messier. The underlying DOM is persistent and mutable. Not only would naive re-rendering be prohibitively costly, but it would also fundamentally break the experience. (Input's losing focus, animations, etc...)

There are ways to mitigate this. We build ever-better constructs in hopes of reshaping our reality to fit. But at some point, we need to separate the implementation from the ideal to be able to talk about these things honestly.

So today we look at Signals as they are and what they have to offer.

Decoupling Performance from Code Organization

This is the moment when you first realize that something really different is going on. There is more to it, though. This is not an invitation to litter your app with global state, but rather a way to illustrate state is independent of components.

function Counter() { console.log("I log once"); const [count, setCount] = createSignal(0); setInterval(() => setCount(count() + 1), 1000); return <div>{count()}</div>
}

Similarly, a console.log that doesn't re-execute when a counter updates is a cute trick but doesn't tell the whole story.

The truth is this behavior persists throughout the whole component tree. State that originates in a parent component and is used in a child doesn't cause the parent or the child to re-run. Only the part of the DOM that depends on it. Prop drilling, Context API, or whatever it is the same thing.

And isn't just about the impact of spreading state changes across components but also multiple states within the same component.

function MoreRealisticComponent(props) { const [selected, setSelected] = createSignal(null); return ( <div> <p>Selected {selected() ? selected().name : 'nothing'}</p>
 <ul> {props.items.map(item => <li> <button onClick={() => setSelected(item)}> {item.name} </button>
 </li>
 )} </ul>
 </div>
 );
}

Porting the example from Svelte Creator Rich Harris' Virtual DOM is Pure Overhead to SolidJS it is important to understand, with Signals, updating the selected state does not cause any execution other than changing the text in that <p>. There is no re-running the list or diffing it. That is even true if the name in one of the rows updates. With Signals, we can directly update the text of that one button.

Note: It is customary in Solid to use <For> component instead of .map when rendering a loop so as to not recreate every row when entries of the items are inserted, removed, or moved.

You might be thinking, "I get it. It's fast. But I've never had trouble ensuring good performance in something like React." But the takeaway is more than that.

You no longer need to be concerned with components for optimal execution.

You can put your whole app in one component or many components and get the same benefit. You can break apart components for your sake. How you want to organize your app. Not because you need to isolate some part of the UI that changes. If performance ever became a concern it wouldn't be due to the structure of your components necessitating costly refactoring.

This is not an insignificant benefit to developer experience.

Separating Dynamic from Static

@thdxr yeah i could see that. my frustration partially stems from — yes, there are a bunch of things where React gets confusing — but this is the clearest win React ever had, if it had one. erasing the distinction between first and next renders propelled it up. sad to see this forgotten

There have been some conversations to suggest this is a bad thing. If you want more perspective see @dan_abramov's response to my previous article.

I don't just want to talk about why this is a good thing, but how it is actually an amazing thing. Being able to optimize for each is beneficial. This is one of those places where aligning with the underlying platform pays off with dividends.

Classically speaking there is a tradeoff with using a distributed event system like Signals vs something that runs top-down. While updates will be quicker for the event system, at creation it has the additional overhead of setting up subscriptions.

This is even compounded by the fact that the web generally is a document-oriented interface. Even in Single Page Apps, you will be doing a lot of navigation which involves a lot of creation.

Makes sense. However, the web platform is aware of this cost and has made it more efficient to create elements in bulk than individually. Extracting the static parts for mass creation makes a bigger impact than those subscriptions.

And the benefits don't stop with the browser. With a Signals-based system the complexity, size, and execution of your code scale with how interactive your UI is rather than how many elements are in it.

Consider a server-rendered page with few interactions. Maybe an eCommerce site. The static parts are server-rendered HTML. You don't even need the code for that to make it interactive. Just delete the static parts from the bundle.

Steve from Builder.io (at 1:16) explains how this works in Qwik:

Admittedly this is mostly a performance concern. It comes from the same motivation for Islands architecture and React Server Components. It addresses a very real pain point we are facing today with the trend towards ever-bigger JavaScript bundles and slow initial page loads.

Overall, my position is this separation leads to a certain amount of transparency. It makes it easier to explain and reason about what is actually going on. While less simple than the ideal, it makes escape hatches, which are an important part of any system, more coherent.

Universalizing the Language of UI

One of the most powerful things about Signals is viewing their impact as a language. And I don't mean a compiled language. Signals are completely a runtime mechanism. There is no magic here. Just a Directed Acyclic Graph.

While it seems clear there is a convergence in concepts of State, Derived State, and Effects, not all the mental models and implementations line up.

Signals are independent of any component or rendering system. And only represent state relationships. Unlike something like React Hooks which have additional primitives to describe how to guard execution like useCallback, React.memo and concepts like stable references(useRef) to handle the coordination of effects.

Both of Dan's articles listed at the bottom of the article are a really good exploration into how to effectively use these primitives in React.

Additionally, Signals lend to traceability. They give you a way of understanding what updates and why.

Release day for @solid_js devtools!0.21.0 adds an essential way of inspecting reactivity: a Dependency Graph — it will display current (direct and indirect) sources and observers of the selected signal or computation

github.com/thetarnav/soli…

They encourage patterns that lead to more declarative code. By making organizing code around data instead of component flow we can see what data is driving change. (Thanks Dan for the example).

// control flow
const count = videos.length;
let heading = emptyHeading;
let somethingElse = 42;
if (count > 0) { const noun = count > 1 ? 'Videos' : 'Video'; heading = count + ' ' + noun; somethingElse = someOtherStuff();
} // derived data
const format = (count) => count > 1 ? 'Videos' : 'Video'; const count = videos.length;
const heading = count > 0 ? format(count) : emptyHeading;
const somethingElse = count > 0 ? someOtherStuff : 42;

It poses an interesting question about the purpose of code. Should we optimize it for making it easier to write or easier to read?

Ok, But What About the Tradeoffs?

There are definitely tradeoffs. The most obvious one is that they make the data special instead of the application of that data. We aren't dealing with plain objects anymore, but with primitives. This is very similar to Promises or Event Emitters. You are reasoning about the data flow rather than the control flow.

JavaScript is not a data flow language so it is possible to lose Reactivity. To be fair this is true of any JavaScript UI library or framework without the aid of tooling or compilation. For Signals, this is more emphasized as where you access the value is important.

I call this Signal's (singular) Hook Rule. There are consequences to this. There is a learning curve. It pushes you to write code a certain way. When using things like Proxies there are additional caveats like certain mechanisms in JavaScript language (like spreading, destructuring) have restricted usage.

Another consideration is around disposal. Subscriptions link both ways so if one side is long-lived it is possible to hold onto memory longer than desired. Modern frameworks are pretty good at handling this disposal automatically but this is inherent to Signal's design.

Finally, historically there were concerns about large uncontrollable graphs. Cycles and unpredictable propagation. These concerns largely are in past due to the work that has been done over the past several years. I'd go as far as these problems are what Signals solve and why you would use them over other message/event systems.

Conclusion

@dan_abramov @RyanCarniato The React thought leadership had long cultivated the narrative of “our model is more correct”, almost becoming a dogma. In reality, many engineers are simply more productive with the signals mental model - arguing on a philosophical level is pointless when that happens.

There are many ways to approach the challenge of creating great user interfaces. I've aimed at keeping this discussion grounded but I think there is a lot to get excited about here.

When you are building with a foundation of primitives there is a lot you can do. The exploration into reducing JavaScript load overhead and incremental interactivity is an area that Signals naturally fits in.

And thing is to use Signals to great benefit you do not need a compiler. Not even for templating. You can use Tagged Template Literals and do it all with no build step. We tend to use compilation though to make ergonomics smoother. But Signals are also a great choice for compilation.

Compilers and language exploration become that much easier when you have efficient building blocks you can target. And that isn't just true of us but for AIs. We've seen this suggested to improve upon everything from using analytics to drive code splitting to optimize initial load to optimizing compilers ability to understand code intent.

What was interesting about the responses to this tweet is that people got hung up with signals and the fact I opted to use React’s APIs.

Signals and React aren’t even that important really. It’s the compiler that is. Imagine a UI compiler that leverages AI to self-improve… twitter.com/trueadm/status…

I’ve been working on a new React-like framework in my spare time. It’s fully compiler driven, there’s no virtual DOM, or component re-rendering. There are signals, but they’re never exposed to the developer and they’re also fully compiled to avoid any runtime lookups. And…

Whether Signals are best suited to be held by developers or to be low-level primitives for machines, they appear to be an important step in the ever-evolving world of web front-end.

Related Resources:

A Hands-on Introduction to Fine-grained Reactivity
The Evolution of Signals in JavaScript
React vs Signals: 10 Years Later
Virtual DOM is Pure Overhead By Rich Harris
Components are Pure Overhead
Metaphysics and JavaScript By Rich Harris
Making setInterval Declarative with React Hooks By Dan Abramov
Before you memo() By Dan Abramov

原文:

In this tutorial, we will build a rock, paper, scissors game with GitHub Copilot. We will also explore GitHub Copilot, a cloud-based AI tool that assists users of various editors in suggesting lines of code and whole functions instantly. We will discuss how Copilot has redefined productivity for millions of developers, and the benefits it provides. This tutorial includes the use of CodeTour, a VS Code extension, that allows developers to create and follow a guided walkthrough of a codebase. Let's get started!

What is GitHub Copilot?

GitHub Copilot has helped redefine productivity for millions of developers by introducing them to the magic of AI assistance. GitHub Copilot is a cloud-based artificial intelligence tool developed by GitHub and OpenAI to assist users of Visual Studio, Visual Studio Code, Neovim, and JetBrains by suggesting lines of code and whole functions instantly. With Copilot, you can write a regular expression or interact with an API for the first time without leaving your editor.

Why I love GitHub Copilot

GitHub Copilot boosts productivity because it helps developers spend more time thinking about the theory they are building than about the code itself.

However, beyond productivity, GitHub Copilot helps provide me with psychological safety. Psychological safety refers to the belief that you can speak up, ask questions, make mistakes, and contribute without fear of punishment or humiliation. When employees feel safe to learn, ask questions, contribute, and lead, it boosts the team’s and individual’s overall confidence, morale, and performance.

Unfortunately, the reality is psychological safety is not always common at work. When I’m feeling uncertain about asking for help, or I’m not even sure how to word my questions, I can use GitHub Copilot to help me:

  • Brainstorm ideas when I reach a mental block
  • Jog my memory
  • Retain focus
  • Determine if I’m going in the right direction

Why would I build a rock, paper, scissors game?

  • I did this for fun
  • I did this to build my Python skills. I’m a baby Pythonista. For the past 5 years, I’ve been writing different forms of JavaScript on the frontend and backend, but ever since I started using GitHub Copilot, I’ve taken up an interest in Python. And I want to continue to read, type, and navigate code written in Python. It’s a less verbose language than JavaScript, so it feels easy for me to learn.
  • I wanted to create a short, fun activity to help folks learn how to leverage GitHub Copilot because it’s not always obvious how to get started. Hint: Provide as much context as possible through comments and lines of code.
  • I remember building a rock, paper, scissors game when I was in a coding bootcamp back in 2018. At the time, the logic seemed so difficult. I’m hoping this can be a helpful tool for developers who are learning to code.
  • I want to continue to creatively use GitHub Copilot for different situations to understand its limitations and strengths.

To complete this tutorial, you will need:

  • Access to GitHub Copilot
  • Open this repository

This is a self-guided tutorial to help folks learn how to use GitHub Copilot. In this project you will find:

  • a main.py file with no contents
  • a devcontainer that installs CodeTour and GitHub Copilot when the Codespace is created
  • a CodeTour to guide the developer through using GitHub Copilot to develop a rock, paper, scissors game.

  1. Your CodeTour will begin! Follow the…

What is CodeTour?

CodeTour is a VS Code extension developed by my amazing coworker, Jonathan Carter! It allows developers to create and follow a guided walkthrough of a codebase.

What you will find in this repository

In this repository, you will find:

  • a main.py file with no contents
  • a devcontainer that installs CodeTour and GitHub Copilot when the Codespace is created
  • a CodeTour to guide the developer through using GitHub Copilot to develop a rock, paper, scissors game.

How to use the guided CodeTour

Navigate to this repository that I created. Then follow the instructions below:

Step 1

Step 2

Choose the “Explorer Icon” on the left sidebar of your editor.

Step 3

Toggle the CodeTour panel

Step 4

Press the “Play button” to start the tour.

Step 5

Your CodeTour will begin! Follow the CodeTour’s steps to learn how to use GitHub Copilot.

Your CodeTour will take you through the following steps

Step 1

Introduction: Hi there! this is a guided tour to help you learn GitHub Copilot. We will build a rock, paper, scissors game with Python.

Step 2

Let's give GitHub Copilot some context about what we're building. Write this comment # Write a rock, paper, scissors, game at the top of your main.py file.

Now, let's prompt Copilot to import the random module. Write this comment # import random module on the next line. Press enter to create a new line and accept Copilot's suggestion.

Step 3

Now, let's prompt Copilot to create a main function that handles the logic of the game with a comment that says # define main function that handles all the logic. Press enter to create a new line and accept Copilot's suggestion.

Step 4

Now, let's prompt Copilot to call the main function. Write this comment # call main function on the next line. Press enter to create a new line and accept Copilot's suggestion.

Step 5

Try it out! Let's run the code to see if it works. In your terminal, run python3 main.py. It should start the rock, paper, scissors game. Mission complete! You've used GitHub Copilot to create a rock, paper, scissors game in Python!

Items.Title: 在 Java 中用枚举替换 If-Else 语句 | Replacing If-Else Statements with Enums in Java
Items.Description:
那篇文章展示了如何使用 Java 中的枚举更改if-else语句的简单方法。 在该教程中，我们将使用一个简单的应用程序，该应用程序仅使用if-else语句编写，并通过使用构造函数创建枚举来删除所有这些语句。

示例程序

我们将在本教程中使用的 Java 程序是一个简单的命令行应用程序，它在用户提供所需消息后返回 HTTP 状态代码。

例如，如果我们用参数“SUCCESS”执行它，那么它的返回值就是“200”。

旧代码

我们的应用程序由多个if-else语句组成。 因此，根据用户的消息，在四种情况下进行测试，定义相应的HTTP状态码返回给用户。

 public class Application { public static void main(String[] args) { if (args.length < 1) { System.out.println("No args provided!"); System.exit(0); } String providedMessage = args[0]; int returnCode = 0; if ("SUCCESS".equalsIgnoreCase(providedMessage)) { returnCode = 200; } else if ("CREATED".equalsIgnoreCase(providedMessage)) { returnCode = 201; } else if ("MOVED".equalsIgnoreCase(providedMessage)) { returnCode = 301; } else if ("UNAUTHORIZED".equalsIgnoreCase(providedMessage)) { returnCode = 401; } System.out.println("Your HTTP code is: " + returnCode); } }

运行该 Java 程序：

 marcos@Desktop: javac Application.java marcos@Desktop: java Application success Your HTTP code is: 200.

重构代码

为了实现这一点，我们可以使用 java ENUMS 的一个属性：构造函数。 使用它，对于为我们的 ENUM 提供的每个值，我们可以定义一个默认返回值。

编写我们的 ENUM

要做的第一个改变是定义我们的枚举类。

 enum HttpCode { SUCCESS, CREATED, MOVED, UNAUTHORIZED; }

尽管声明了该类，但我们不允许使用它来替换我们的if-else语句，因为 Java 不知道在我们的枚举HttpCode实例化后需要返回的代码。

这可以通过为HttpCode定义一个构造函数来非常简单地调整。

 enum HttpCode { SUCCESS(200), CREATED(201), MOVED(301), UNAUTHORIZED(401); private int code; HttpCode(int code) { this.code = code; } public int getCode(){ return this.code; } }

请注意，在我们的枚举类中创建了一个字段code 。 由于HttpCode的每个选项都有为构造函数定义的默认状态，因此在使用HttpCode.valueOf(String message)方法实例化后，其中一个默认值将存储在字段code中，可以通过HttpCode.getCode() 。

调整主要方法

现在我们有了一个枚举类，我们只需要改变我们原来的main代码来使用它。

 public static void main(String[] args) { if (args.length < 1) { System.out.println("No args provided!"); System.exit(0); } String providedMessage = args[0]; HttpCode httpStatusCode = HttpCode.valueOf(providedMessage.toUpperCase()); System.out.println("Your HTTP code is: " + httpStatusCode.getCode()); }

完毕！ 现在，我们的应用程序可以为用户返回所提供消息的 HTTP 状态代码。 以下代码显示了我们的整个类，已经重构。

 public class Application { public static void main(String[] args) { if (args.length < 1) { System.out.println("No args provided!"); System.exit(0); } String providedMessage = args[0]; HttpCode httpStatusCode = HttpCode.valueOf(providedMessage.toUpperCase()); System.out.println("Your HTTP code is: " + httpStatusCode.getCode()); } } enum HttpCode { SUCCESS(200), CREATED(201), MOVED(301), UNAUTHORIZED(401); private int code; HttpCode(int code) { this.code = code; } public int getCode() { return this.code; } }

运行该 Java 程序：

 marcos@Desktop: javac Application.java marcos@Desktop: java Application UNAUTHORIZED Your HTTP code is: 401.

在这种情况下，我们首先想到的是：“好吧，我删除了我的语句，但我也改进了我的代码行数！”。 事实上，我们的应用程序似乎要大得多，但在实际情况下，这可以减少行数，因为 ENUM 可以在我们代码的其他部分重用。

此外，使代码更具可读性，因为具有多个验证的 if-else 结构不复存在，Java 开始为我们进行此验证。

另一个好处是我们可以在枚举的类中添加更多选项，而无需在所有状态代码验证发生时进行更改，因为它只存在于创建的枚举中。

结论

这是一个简单但不太适用的示例。 但是，它可以展示使用枚举的更好方法。 同样重要的是要注意：不要在没有考虑后果的情况下更改您看到的每个if-else语句。 这是关于如何以其他方式做事的想法，每个场景都不同，您必须检查，同意；D？

就这样！
请在评论中留下您的建议、更正和测试。 感谢您阅读 :D。



原文:

That post shows a simple way of how if-else statements can be changed with enums in Java. On that tutorial, we will use a simple application which was written solely using if-else statements, and remove all of them by creating an enum with constructor.

The example program

The Java program we are going to use for this tutorial is a simple command-line application that returns a HTTP status code after the user provides a required message.

For example, if we execute it with the argument "SUCCESS", then its return is "200".

The old code

Our application is composed of several if-else statements. Therefore, basing on user's message, it is tested on four conditions to define the corresponding HTTP status code and return it to the user.

public class Application { public static void main(String[] args) { if (args.length < 1) { System.out.println("No args provided!"); System.exit(0); } String providedMessage = args[0]; int returnCode = 0; if ("SUCCESS".equalsIgnoreCase(providedMessage)) { returnCode = 200; } else if ("CREATED".equalsIgnoreCase(providedMessage)) { returnCode = 201; } else if ("MOVED".equalsIgnoreCase(providedMessage)) { returnCode = 301; } else if ("UNAUTHORIZED".equalsIgnoreCase(providedMessage)) { returnCode = 401; } System.out.println("Your HTTP code is: " + returnCode); }
}

Running that Java program:

marcos@Desktop: javac Application.java
marcos@Desktop: java Application success
Your HTTP code is: 200.

The refactored code

To make that possible, we can use a property of java ENUMS: the constructors. Using it, for each value provided for our ENUM, we can define a default return value.

Writing our ENUM

The first change to be done is define our enum class.

enum HttpCode { SUCCESS, CREATED, MOVED, UNAUTHORIZED; }

As much as the class is declared, we are not allowed to use it for replace our if-else statements, since Java will not know the code that needs to be returned after the instantiation of our enum HttpCode.

This can be ajusted very simply, by defining a constructor for HttpCode.

enum HttpCode { SUCCESS(200), CREATED(201), MOVED(301), UNAUTHORIZED(401); private int code; HttpCode(int code) { this.code = code; } public int getCode(){ return this.code; }
}

Notice that a field code was created inside our enum class. Since each options of HttpCode has a default status defined for the contructor, after an instantiation using the method HttpCode.valueOf(String message), one of the default values will be stored at the field code, which can be obtained by the method HttpCode.getCode().

Adjusting the main method

Now that we have an enum class, we just need to change our original main code to use it.

public static void main(String[] args) { if (args.length < 1) { System.out.println("No args provided!"); System.exit(0); } String providedMessage = args[0]; HttpCode httpStatusCode = HttpCode.valueOf(providedMessage.toUpperCase()); System.out.println("Your HTTP code is: " + httpStatusCode.getCode());
}

Done! Now, our application can return for user a HTTP status code for a provided message. The following code shows our entire class, already refactored.

public class Application { public static void main(String[] args) { if (args.length < 1) { System.out.println("No args provided!"); System.exit(0); } String providedMessage = args[0]; HttpCode httpStatusCode = HttpCode.valueOf(providedMessage.toUpperCase()); System.out.println("Your HTTP code is: " + httpStatusCode.getCode()); }
} enum HttpCode { SUCCESS(200), CREATED(201), MOVED(301), UNAUTHORIZED(401); private int code; HttpCode(int code) { this.code = code; } public int getCode() { return this.code; }
}

Running that Java program:

marcos@Desktop: javac Application.java
marcos@Desktop: java Application UNAUTHORIZED
Your HTTP code is: 401.

The first thing we can think on that case is: "Okay, I removed my statements, but I also improved the quantity of lines of my code!". In the truth, it seems that our application is much bigger, but on real cases, that can decrease the quantity of lines, since the ENUM can be reused on another parts of our code.

Also, makes the code more readable, since the if-else structures, with several validations, ceases to exist, and Java starts to do this verification for us.

Another good thing is that we can add more options at enums's class without the need of change at all occurrences of status code validations, giving it only exists in the enum that was created.

Conclusion

This is a simple, and not that much applicable, example. However, it can demonstrate a better way to use enums. It's also important to note: do not change every if-else statement you see without think on consequences. This is an idea of how to do things on other way, every scenario is different, you must check, agreed ;D?

That's all!
Please, leave your suggestions, corrections and tests on comments. Thank you for reading :D.

Items.Description:
卡米洛·马丁内斯

发表于 3 月 4 日

一起听这些信之前和之后有一个： ZSH 。 您可能想知道：为什么我以前没有听说过这个？ 哦！

但是当你发现Oh My ZSH 时，事情会变得更好！ 一个 ZSH 框架，可以提高您的工作效率并改善您的工作流程。

所以为什么？

  • 不再有 cd（更改目录）命令。 只需使用：
命令结果
folder/ 以/结尾的文件夹名称
.. 返回一个文件夹（父目录）
... 返回两个文件夹（父目录中的父文件夹）
...... 返回五个文件夹
/ 去根
~ 回家
- 跳转到上一条路径
  • 命令结果take <file> 下载 gzip 文件（ .gz 、 .bz2 、 .xz ）并解压take <git_repo> 从 url ( httl , ssh ) 克隆 git repo take <folder> 新建一个文件夹

  • 使用-在上一条路径和当前路径之间跳转（就像电视遥控器一样）

  • 例如，键入ls -并按TAB将列出所有命令的选项，以及对它们的作用的有用描述

  • 别名命令。 使用alias列出所有内容或使用grep过滤它，例如alias | grep git alias | grep git

  • 使用 globbing（Zsh 的一项功能），您可以列出具有特定扩展名的文件。 例如， ls *.html将列出当前目录中的所有 HTML 文件。 要包含子目录，请更改为： ls **/*.html

  • 递归路径扩展。 拼写更正和近似完成，在键入目录名称时出现小错误时自动更正

  • 历史子字符串搜索编写命令并按向上箭头循环以前的用法

  • 自动完成，使用选项卡在选项之间跳转，然后按回车键进行选择。 使用目录、文件和命令

  • 使用!运行历史命令后跟历史记录中的数字，例如!137

  • 进入!! 将调出最后一条命令。 如果命令失败，这很方便，因为它需要管理员权限。 在这种情况下，您可以键入sudo !!

插件

有很多插件可以使用。 建议探索选项并使用适合您需求的选项。

我的建议是：

  • 我从外部存储库的插件中获得了另一个额外的功能来添加更多功能：

    主题

    也有很多主题，但我最喜欢的是Powerlevel10k ，因为它易于设置和使用

    混帐

    部分意义master 当前分支#v1 在分支上时未显示@5fc6fca4 当前提交； 在分支或标签上时不显示⇣1 本地分支落后于远程 1 次提交⇡2 本地分支领先远程 2 次提交⇠3 本地分支落后于远程推送 3 次提交⇢4 本地分支领先于远程推送 4 次提交*5 有5个藏品merge 合并正在进行中（可能是其他操作） ~6 有6个合并冲突+7 有7个阶段性变化!8 有 8 个未暂存的更改?9 有 9 个未跟踪的文件

捷径

编辑长命令

您可以设置您喜欢的编辑器，例如运行export EDITOR="code -w"以使用 VSCode

停车命令

运行该其他命令后，原始命令将取消停放并重新填充命令行，以便您可以继续。

如果您忘记在命令之前执行命令，这很有用。

显示别名

路径历史

要查看列表，请键入dirs -v 。 通过键入~#切换到此列表中的任何目录，其中#是列表中目录的编号。

挂钩

您可以使用 ZSH Hooks 个性化操作（在命令之前和之后）

来源

原文:

Camilo Martinez

Posted on Mar 4

   

There is a before and after listening to these letters together: ZSH. You may wonder: Why did not I hear about this before? D'oh!

ZSH (Z Shell) it's a real evolution, modernize terminal things with simple solutions. But the thing getting better when you discover Oh My ZSH! a framework for ZSH that boost your productivity and improve your workflow.

So, why?

  • No more cd (change directory) command. Just use:
Command Result
folder/ write the folder name with / at the end
.. go back one folder (parent dir)
... go back two folders (parent from parent dir)
...... go back five folders
/ go to root
~ go to home
- jump to previous path
  • take the 3 in 1 command who create a directory and automatically change the path to it
Command Result
take <file> Download gzip file (.gz, .bz2, .xz) and uncompress
take <git_repo> Clone git repo from url (httl, ssh)
take <folder> Create a new folder

  • Jump between last and current path with - (like a TV remote control)

  • zsh_stats will give you a list of the top 20 commands and how many times they've been run

  • Tab completion is another great feature. For example, typing ls - and pressing TAB will list all of the command's options, along with a helpful description of what they do

  • Alias commands. List all with alias or filter it with grep for example alias | grep git

  • With globbing (a Zsh feature), you can list files with a particular extension. For example, ls *.html will list all HTML files in the current directory. To include subdirectories, change to: ls **/*.html

  • Recursive path expansion. /u/lo/b expands to /user/local/bin

  • Spelling correction and approximate completion, automatic correct when having minor mistake typing a directory name

  • History substring search writing a command and pressing the up arrow cycles through previous usages

  • Autocomplete, jump between options with tab, and press return for selection. Works with directories, files, and commands

  • Run history command with ! followed by the number in history, like !137

  • Entering !! will bring up the last command. This is handy if a command fails because it needs admin rights. In this case you can type sudo !!

Plugins

There are a lot of plugins to use. It's recommended to explore the options and use what is good for your needs.

My recommendations are:

  • sudo to easily prefix your current or previous commands with sudo by pressing esc twice
  • command-not-found to provide suggested packages to be installed if a command cannot be found
  • git provides many aliases and a few useful functions
  • history-substring-search a clean-room implementation of the Fish shell's history search feature, where you can type in any part of any command from history and then press chosen keys, such as the UP and DOWN arrows, to cycle through matches
  • web-search adds aliases for searching with Google, Wiki, Bing, YouTube and other popular services
  • z command that tracks your most visited directories and allows you to access them with very few keystrokes

I've another extra from plugins from external repositories to add more functionalities:

Themes

There are also a lot of themes, but my favorite is Powerlevel10k because is easy to set up and use

Git

Incredible and complete aliases for Git

Git Status Prompt can contain the following bits:

segment meaning
master current branch
#v1 HEAD is tagged with v1; not shown when on a branch
@5fc6fca4 current commit; not shown when on a branch or tag
⇣1 local branch is behind the remote by 1 commit
⇡2 local branch is ahead of the remote by 2 commits
⇠3 local branch is behind the push remote by 3 commits
⇢4 local branch is ahead of the push remote by 4 commits
*5 there are 5 stashes
merge merge is in progress (could be some other action)
~6 there are 6 merge conflicts
+7 there are 7 staged changes
!8 there are 8 unstaged changes
?9 there are 9 untracked files

Shortcuts

Edit long command

ctrl+x+e open the command on an editor to easy edit, once save and close the editor it will be updated on the terminal

You can set your favorite editor, for example run export EDITOR="code -w" to use VSCode

Park a command

ctrl+q "parks" the command you're currently typing and takes you back to the prompt, letting you start over and type another command. Once you run that other command, the original command is un-parked and refills the command line so you can continue.

This is good for if you, say, forgot to do a command before a command.

Reveal alias

ctrl+x a will transform the alias to the real command

Path history

ZSH keeps the history of directories you visited so you can quickly switch to any of them.

To see the list, type dirs -v. Switch to any directory in this list by typing ~# where # is the number of the directory in the list.

Hooks

You can personalize actions (before and after a command) with ZSH Hooks

Sources

That’s All Folks!
Happy Coding 🖖

Items.Description:
介绍

它由 Vercel（以前称为 Zeit）开发，正在成为前端开发的流行选择。 在这篇文章中，我们将仔细研究 Next.js 是什么、它是如何工作的，以及为什么它是一个如此有价值的 Web 开发工具。

什么是 Next.js？

它提供了一组功能和工具，可以更轻松地构建可扩展、快速且高效的 Web 应用程序。 服务器端渲染 (SSR)

  • 自动代码拆分
  • 易于使用的路由
  • 内置性能优化
  • 支持打字稿

    • 它负责构建快速高效的网站所需的大量繁重工作，并为开发人员提供一组工具和组件，使其更容易上手。

    这意味着该框架将为服务器上的页面生成 HTML 并将其发送到浏览器，在那里它将呈现为一个功能齐全的页面。 这有几个好处，包括更快的页面加载时间、改进的 SEO 以及更好的用户可访问性。

    这意味着该框架会自动将您的代码分解为更小、更易于管理的块，然后在用户与页面交互时按需加载。 这有助于减少初始 JavaScript 负载的大小，从而显着提高网站或应用程序的性能。

    为什么选择 Next.js？

    出于多种原因，Next.js 是 Web 开发的重要工具。 首先，它可以更轻松地构建快速高效的网站，这对用户和搜索引擎都很重要。 其次，它提供了一组工具和组件，可以更轻松地开始 Web 开发，即使对于那些刚接触该领域的人也是如此。 最后，Next.js 是一个积极维护和良好支持的框架，这意味着开发人员可以依靠它随着时间的推移不断发展和改进。

    您可以使用 Next.js 构建什么？

    以下是一些可以使用 Next.js 构建的示例：

    1. 电子商务网站：Next.js 非常适合构建需要高度交互性、动态内容和 SEO 优化的电子商务网站。

    2. 社交媒体平台：Next.js 可用于创建需要实时更新、用户身份验证和高级 UI 组件的社交媒体平台。

    3. 渐进式网络应用程序：Next.js 可用于创建离线工作的渐进式网络应用程序，提供类似原生的体验，并具有更快的加载时间。

    4. 内容驱动的网站：Next.js 可用于创建高度依赖内容的网站，例如新闻网站、博客和信息门户。

    5. 企业应用程序：Next.js 可用于构建需要高安全性、可扩展性和性能的企业级应用程序。

    6. 着陆页：Next.js 可用于构建需要快速加载时间、SEO 优化和高级 A/B 测试功能的着陆页。

    以下是 Next.js 有助于改善用户体验的一些方法：

    1. 更快的加载时间：Next.js 使用服务器端渲染 (SSR) 和自动代码拆分，可以显着减少初始加载时间并提高整体性能。

    2. 改进的 SEO：Next.js 提供内置的 SEO 优化功能，例如自动生成元标记和站点地图，这有助于提高搜索引擎排名并增加网站访问量。

    3. 动态内容：Next.js 允许轻松集成动态内容，例如用户生成的数据、实时更新和交互功能，这可以提高参与度和用户满意度。

    4. 移动响应能力：Next.js 提供响应式设计功能，确保 Web 应用程序适合移动设备并针对不同设备进行优化，从而改善跨多个平台的可访问性和用户体验。

    5. 安全性：Next.js 提供了内置的安全功能，例如 XSS 保护和自动 CSRF 保护，这有助于防止安全漏洞并增强用户信任。

    这包括服务器端渲染、自动代码拆分和易于使用的路由等功能，这些功能不属于 React 本身。

    正如我们所讨论的，Next.js 是一种用于构建 Web 应用程序的强大工具，但与任何技术一样，它也有其优点和缺点。 以下是使用 Next.js 的一些优点和缺点：

    优点：

    1. 服务端渲染（SSR）：Next.js 使用 SSR，可以提高你的 web 应用程序的性能和速度，尤其是对于首次加载。

    2. 易于使用：Next.js 建立在 React 之上，因此如果您有 React 经验，您会发现 Next.js 很容易上手。

    3. 多才多艺：Next.js 多才多艺，可用于构建范围广泛的 Web 应用程序，从小型登陆页面到大型企业应用程序。

    4. 快速开发：Next.js 带有许多内置功能和实用程序，例如自动代码拆分，可以帮助加快开发速度并提高生产力。

    缺点：

    1. 学习曲线：虽然 Next.js 是建立在 React 之上的，但它有自己的概念和特性，可能需要一些时间来学习和掌握。

    2. 有限的自定义：Next.js 有自己的文件结构和路由系统，这会限制您在 Web 应用程序中可以实现的自定义级别。

    3. 复杂性：Next.js 可能很复杂，尤其是对于初学者来说，因为它有许多特性和实用程序。

    总而言之， Next.js 是一个强大而灵活的工具，可以极大地改善 Web 应用程序的开发体验和用户体验。 它提供了许多内置功能和实用程序，例如服务器端渲染、SEO 优化和自动代码拆分，有助于加快开发速度和提高性能。 虽然 Next.js 有其优点和缺点，但对于希望构建动态且引人入胜的 Web 应用程序（从电子商务网站到渐进式 Web 应用程序）的开发人员来说，它是一个不错的选择。 有了这份面向初学者的综合指南，开发人员可以轻松开始使用 Next.js 并利用其众多功能来构建高质量和响应迅速的 Web 应用程序。

    原文:

    Introduction

    Next.js is a popular React-based framework that makes it easier to build fast and efficient websites and applications. It was developed by Vercel (formerly known as Zeit) and is becoming a popular choice for front-end development. In this post, we’ll take a closer look at what Next.js is, how it works, and why it’s such a valuable tool for web development.

    What is Next.js?

    Next.js is a JavaScript framework that is built on top of React. It provides a set of features and tools that make it easier to build scalable, fast, and efficient web applications. Some of the key features of Next.js include:

    • Server-side rendering (SSR)
    • Automatic code splitting
    • Easy-to-use routing
    • Build-in optimization for performance
    • Support for Typescript

    How does Next.js work?

    Next.js works by abstracting away a lot of the complexity that comes with building modern web applications. It takes care of a lot of the heavy lifting that is required for building a fast and efficient website and provides developers with a set of tools and components that make it easier to get started.

    One of the key features of Next.js is server-side rendering (SSR). This means that the framework will generate the HTML for a page on the server and send it to the browser, where it will be rendered as a fully functional page. This has several benefits, including faster page load times, improved SEO, and better accessibility for users.

    Another key feature of Next.js is automatic code splitting. This means that the framework will automatically break down your code into smaller, more manageable chunks, which are then loaded on demand as the user interacts with the page. This helps to reduce the size of the initial JavaScript payload, which can significantly improve the performance of your website or application.

    Why Next.js?

    Next.js is a valuable tool for web development for several reasons. Firstly, it makes it easier to build fast and efficient websites, which is important for both users and search engines. Secondly, it provides a set of tools and components that make it easier to get started with web development, even for those who are new to the field. Finally, Next.js is an actively maintained and well-supported framework, which means that developers can rely on it to continue to evolve and improve over time.

    What Can you Build with Next.js?

    Next.js is a powerful and flexible tool that allows developers to build a wide range of web applications. Here are some examples of what can be built with Next.js:

    1. E-commerce websites: Next.js is well-suited for building e-commerce websites that require a high degree of interactivity, dynamic content, and SEO optimization.

    2. Social media platforms: Next.js can be used to create social media platforms that require real-time updates, user authentication, and advanced UI components.

    3. Progressive web apps: Next.js can be used to create progressive web apps that work offline, offer native-like experiences, and have faster load times.

    4. Content-driven websites: Next.js can be used to create websites that rely heavily on content, such as news sites, blogs, and information portals.

    5. Enterprise applications: Next.js can be used to build enterprise-level applications that require high security, scalability, and performance.

    6. Landing pages: Next.js can be used to build landing pages that require fast load times, SEO optimization, and advanced A/B testing capabilities.

    Next.js and User Experience?

    Next.js can greatly improve user experience (UX) in web applications. Here are some ways Next.js can contribute to a better user experience:

    1. Faster load times: Next.js uses server-side rendering (SSR) and automatic code splitting, which can significantly reduce initial load times and improve overall performance.

    2. Improved SEO: Next.js provides built-in SEO optimization features, such as the automatic generation of meta tags and sitemaps, which can help improve search engine rankings and increase traffic to your website.

    3. Dynamic content: Next.js allows for easy integration of dynamic content, such as user-generated data, real-time updates, and interactive features, which can enhance engagement and user satisfaction.

    4. Mobile responsiveness: Next.js provides responsive design features that ensure that web applications are mobile-friendly and optimized for different devices, improving accessibility and user experience across multiple platforms.

    5. Security: Next.js provides built-in security features, such as XSS protection and automatic CSRF protection, which can help prevent security breaches and enhance user trust.

    How is Next.js Different from React?

    Next.js is built on top of React, but it provides a lot more functionality than React alone. While React is a library for building user interfaces, Next.js provides a complete framework for building web applications. This includes features such as server-side rendering, automatic code splitting, and easy-to-use routing, which are not part of React itself.

    Next.js Pros and Cons?

    As we discussed Next.js is a powerful tool for building web applications, but like any technology, it has its pros and cons. Here are some of the advantages and disadvantages of using Next.js:

    Pros:

    1. Server-side rendering (SSR): Next.js uses SSR, which can improve the performance and speed of your web application, especially for the first-time load.

    2. SEO optimization: Next.js provides built-in SEO optimization features, such as the automatic generation of meta tags and sitemaps, which can help improve search engine rankings and increase traffic to your website.

    3. Easy to use: Next.js is built on top of React, so if you have experience with React, you will find it easy to get started with Next.js.

    4. Versatile: Next.js is versatile and can be used to build a wide range of web applications, from small landing pages to large-scale enterprise applications.

    5. Fast development: Next.js comes with many built-in features and utilities, such as automatic code splitting, that can help speed u development and improve productivity.

    Cons:

    1. Learning curve: Although Next.js is built on top of React, it has its own concepts and features that may take some time to learn and master.

    2. Limited customization: Next.js has its own file structure and routing system, which can limit the level of customization that you can achieve in your web application.

    3. Complexity: Next.js can be complex, especially for beginners, due to its many features and utilities.

    In conclusion, Next.js is a powerful and flexible tool that can greatly improve the development experience and user experience of web applications. It provides many built-in features and utilities, such as server-side rendering, SEO optimization, and automatic code splitting, that can help speed up development and improve performance. While Next.js has its pros and cons, it can be a great choice for developers looking to build dynamic and engaging web applications, from e-commerce websites to progressive web apps. With this comprehensive guide for beginners, developers can easily get started with Next.js and take advantage of its many features to build high-quality and responsive web applications.

Items.Description:
作为一名开发人员，拥有合适的工具可以显着提高您的工作质量和工作流程的效率。 有这么多可用的工具，要确定哪些最适合您的需求可能具有挑战性。 在本文中，我们将探讨每个开发人员都应该考虑使用的一些顶级工具。

集成开发环境 (IDE)

一些流行的 IDE 包括 Visual Studio Code、IntelliJ IDEA 和 Eclipse。 这些工具提供了一组强大的功能，例如语法突出显示、代码完成和调试功能。 对于对 vs code 扩展感兴趣的人，我之前分享过一篇关于有用的扩展的文章。

困难时的帮手

在某些情况下，您无法自行解决问题。 在这种情况下，Stackoverflow、chatGPT 和阅读文档可以节省大量时间。 我已经在我以前的一篇文章中讨论过 chatGPT 和 StackOverflow。

创建屏幕截图并提供反馈

简单的界面，没有什么用处，重量轻。

版本控制系统

版本控制系统（例如 Git）允许开发人员管理他们的代码更改并与他人协作。 这些工具跟踪对代码库所做的所有更改，并提供一种在必要时恢复到以前版本的方法。 此外，版本控制系统使团队成员能够同时处理代码库的不同部分，而不会干扰彼此的工作。

包管理器

包管理器（例如 npm 和 pip）使开发人员可以轻松地在其项目中安装、管理和更新依赖项。 这些工具通过自动安装所需的依赖项来简化设置和配置新项目的过程。 此外，包管理器提供了一个集中的包存储库，使发现新库和工具变得容易。

调试工具

这些工具允许开发人员逐行检查代码并实时检查变量和其他数据结构。 此外，调试工具可以帮助识别性能问题并优化代码以提高效率。

任务运行器

任务运行器，例如 Gulp 和 Grunt，在开发过程中自动执行重复性任务。 这些工具可以自动执行缩小、编译和测试等任务，从而为开发人员腾出时间来处理更复杂的任务。 此外，任务运行器提供了一种标准化跨团队和项目的开发流程的方法。

语法纠正器

通过使用各种聊天工具与客户或团队成员沟通，不要害怕犯任何拼写错误。 在这种情况下，你会看起来更专业，为了实现这一点，我使用了两个 chrome 扩展程序 Grammarly 和 Deep L。Grammarly 可以纠正错误，而 Deep L 可以帮助翻译，因为它比谷歌翻译更准确。

编程趋势

为了始终保持最新，重要的是要遵循编程趋势，不要落后。 在这方面，作为开发者，根据市场需求去适应新技术，你会更有价值。 例如，如果我只是因为喜欢而坚持使用 Pascal，或者甚至可能是一种较旧的编程语言，那么我今天将没有任何价值。 为了解决这个问题，我在社交媒体上与开发人员联系。 他们谈论最新的趋势、资源和经验。 第二种解决方案是我最喜欢的， daily.dev开发者新闻在一个地方。 每个人都会发现一些有趣和有用的东西。 第三个解决方案是拥有正确利益的正确社区。 对于开发人员： hashnode 、 medium 、 dev.to 。

文档/指南

为了使指南/文档更有效，请使用 chrome 扩展程序，如Scribe / Tango

协作工具

这些工具提供了一种分享想法、分配任务和实时跟踪进度的方法。 因此，我建议您花一些时间试用这些工具。 它肯定会对您的大部分工作有用。

总之，拥有正确的工具可以对开发人员的生产力和工作质量产生重大影响。 无论您喜欢强大的 IDE 还是轻量级的代码编辑器，总有一款工具可以满足您的需求。 此外，协作和自动化工具可以简化开发流程并实现团队成员之间的有效沟通。 通过使用这些工具，开发人员可以专注于他们最擅长的事情——编写出色的代码。

As a developer, having the right tools can make a significant difference in the quality of your work and the efficiency of your workflow. With so many tools available, it can be challenging to determine which ones are the best fit for your needs. In this article, we will explore some of the top tools that every developer should consider using.

Integrated Development Environments (IDEs)

IDEs are software applications that provide a comprehensive environment for coding, debugging and testing. Some popular IDEs include Visual Studio Code, IntelliJ IDEA, and Eclipse. These tools provide a robust set of features, such as syntax highlighting, code completion, and debugging capabilities. IDEs are also highly customizable, allowing developers to tailor their environment to their specific needs. For people who are interested in the vs code extensions, one of my previous articles I shared about useful extensions.

Helper When You Are Stuck

There are situations when solving issues by Yourself is not possible. In this case, Stackoverflow, chatGPT, and reading documentation can save a lot of time. I already discussed chatGPT and StackOverflow in one of my previous articles.

Create Screenshot and Provide Feedback

Lightshot is the fastest way to take a customizable screenshot. Simple interface, nothing useless and light weight.

Version Control Systems

Version control systems, such as Git, allow developers to manage their code changes and collaborate with others. These tools keep track of all changes made to the codebase and provide a way to revert to previous versions if necessary. Additionally, version control systems enable team members to work on different parts of the codebase simultaneously without interfering with each other’s work.

Package Managers

Package managers, such as npm and pip, make it easy for developers to install, manage, and update dependencies in their projects. These tools simplify the process of setting up and configuring new projects by automating the installation of required dependencies. Additionally, package managers provide a centralized repository of packages, making it easy to discover new libraries and tools.

Debugging Tools

Debugging tools, such as Chrome DevTools and PyCharm, are essential for identifying and fixing errors in code. These tools allow developers to step through the code line by line and inspect variables and other data structures in real-time. Additionally, debugging tools can help identify performance issues and optimize code for better efficiency.

Task Runners

Task runners, such as Gulp and Grunt, automate repetitive tasks in the development process. These tools can automate tasks such as minification, compilation, and testing, freeing up developer time for more complex tasks. Additionally, task runners provide a way to standardize development processes across teams and projects.

Grammar Correctors

Communicate with clients or team members by using various chats without being afraid to make any typo mistakes. In this case, You will look more professional, to achieve this I use two chrome extensions Grammarly and Deep L. Grammarly corrects mistakes and Deep L helps with translations because works more accurately than google translate.

Programming Trends

To be always up to date it is important to follow programming trends to not be behind. In this, as a developer, You will have more worth by adapting to new technologies according to market needs. For example, if I would stick to only pascal, or maybe even an older programming language only because I like it, I would not have any value today. To solve this problem I connect with developers on social media. They talk about the newest trends, resources and experiences. The second solution is my favorite, daily.dev developer news in one place. Everyone will find something interesting and useful. The third solution is the right community with the right interests. For developers: hashnode, medium, dev.to.

Documentation / Guides

To make guides/documentation more effective use chrome extensions like Scribe / Tango

Collaboration Tools

Collaboration tools, such as Slack, Jira, and Trello, enable team members to communicate and collaborate effectively. These tools provide a way to share ideas, assign tasks, and track progress in real-time. For this reason, I would recommend spending some time trying out these tools. It definitely will be useful for most of Your work.

In conclusion, having the right tools can make all the difference in a developer’s productivity and the quality of their work. Whether you prefer a robust IDE or a lightweight code editor, there is a tool available to fit your needs. Additionally, collaboration and automation tools can streamline development processes and enable effective communication among team members. By using these tools, developers can focus on what they do best — writing great code.

Items.Description:
巴达尔亚雅尔

发表于 3 月 4 日

它是桌面环境的一部分，改善了我们与系统的交互。

寻找最好的 Windows 管理器完全是主观的，取决于用户的需求。 它还通过在键盘快捷键、鼠标或自动化脚本的帮助下以合适的方式组织窗口来增强多任务处理的潜力。 它还可以正确使用屏幕上的空间。

与桌面环境不同，窗口管理器只是一个软件。 一般而言，有两种类型的窗口管理器，即平铺式和堆叠式。 平铺是指将窗口相互平铺并有助于避免空白的过程，而堆叠是指将窗口相互堆叠或能够将它们移动到屏幕上任何位置的过程。

窗口管理器充当显示服务器，它是 Linux 内核和图形界面（如 KDE plasma、Aqua 和 Gnome Shell）之间的桥梁。 一些常见的 Linux 窗口管理器是 Awesome、Compiz、Openbox 和 i3。

选择最好的窗口管理器取决于个人喜好，但在了解窗口管理器之前，我们需要了解可在 Linux 中使用的不同桌面环境。 但是，下面讨论了一些流行的桌面环境。

它支持自定义主题。 它具有简洁直观的界面，并提供虚拟工作区和内置屏幕录像机等功能。 它被视为每个人的平铺窗口管理器。

它提供了一个可定制且视觉上吸引人的界面。 它还提供了一系列功能，如虚拟桌面、小部件和桌面效果。 它也可以被视为一个内置窗口管理器的完整桌面环境。

它是低规格系统的理想选择，并提供简单的交互式界面。 它提供了桌面环境，这也有助于在工作中发挥创造力和生产力。 它是一个免费的开源窗口管理器，可与自己的桌面环境一起使用。

现在，我们了解了 Linux 中使用的流行桌面环境，我们可以了解一些与之相关的流行窗口管理器。 他们之中有一些是

因此，它可以为开发人员和用户高度定制。 它已经发展成为一个成熟的 Linux 窗口管理器。 它提供简单快速的窗口管理。 它还使用 XCM 库而不是 XLIB，这有助于控制我们系统中的延迟。

它简单而有效，具有最小的用户界面。 由于这个原因，它可以说比 Awesome 和 xmonad 等其他平铺窗口管理器更好。 但是，没有配置文件，因此对于某些用户来说可能有点复杂。

它是使用 C 编程语言构建的。 它是高度可定制的，因为它具有不同的窗口组织设置来修改元素。 它可以通过纯文本进行配置。

它是使用 Haskell 编程语言构建的。 可以使用配置文件对其进行个性化设置。 它为用户自动安排了大部分窗口。

冰WM：
它是使用 C++ 编程语言构建的。 它适用于轻型机器，因为它极简主义，而且不会影响基本功能、可用性和可定制性。 它使用纯文本来配置和自定义功能。

它具有类似于 Xmonad 和 DWM 的功能。 因此，它快速、简洁、紧凑。 在其中，我们可以在加载程序时编辑和重新加载配置，并且可以在不退出系统的情况下查看配置文件。

它是使用 C++ 编程语言构建的。 它重量轻，因此速度快且响应迅速。 由于上述原因，它是最好的窗口管理器之一。

它是高度可配置的，并使用 Glib 和 Xlib。 它基于将帧拆分为子帧，子帧可以进一步拆分并填充窗口。 因此，它很好地利用了屏幕。

它是一个终端多路复用器，我们可以在其中创建多个终端会话，我们可以从一个屏幕访问和控制这些会话。 在这里，我们还可以在多个用例的不同会话之间共享我们的 shell 实例。

它是使用 Python 构建和配置的。 它是一个易于使用、可扩展和高度可定制的窗口管理器。 除了这些，您还可以在此处远程编写脚本以设置工作区、操作窗口或更新状态栏小部件。

启示：
使用它的好处是您不需要学习或了解任何编程语言来设置环境。 它也被称为桌面环境，区别于窗口管理器。

它是一个开源的轻量级窗口管理器。 它是使用 c 编程语言构建的，因此经过高度优化，即使在较旧或功能较弱的计算机上也能很好地工作。 默认情况下，它只能在 Xlib 库上运行，但可以自定义它以与其他库一起使用，例如用于形状扩展的 libXext、用于 JPEG 背景和图标的 libjpeg、用于图标和背景的 libpng PNG 背景和图标、用于图标和背景的 Cairo 和 libRSVG，以及还有很多。

摇摆：
它与 Wayland i3 兼容，并自动排列应用程序窗口以最大化屏幕空间。 默认情况下，它将窗口组织成网格。 但是，您可以垂直、水平、堆叠或选项卡式地组织应用程序窗口。 您还可以更改窗口的大小或将窗口拆分为多个窗口的容器。 其他功能，例如支持键盘快捷键、使用 Wayland 而不是 Xorg 以及差距。 它支持 Wayland i3 中包含的大部分命令。

该项目旨在构建一个具有最少图形和窗口装饰的简单程序。 相反，它仿照在虚拟终端社区中流行的 GNU Screen。 其高级功能包括拆分窗口，您可以将其组织成不重叠的框架。 您还可以最大化每个框架内的所有窗口。 此外，Ratpoison 仅使用键盘命令进行操作。

对于那些与多个应用程序和多个显示屏交互的人来说，它们可能是一个福音。 但是，在选择窗口管理器之前，我们必须考虑显示环境。 大多数窗口管理器都提供类似的功能，但不同之处仅在于与用户的交互。

如果您刚开始使用窗口管理器，我建议您最好的选择是 i3 或 xmonad，因为它们速度快，设计精简，并提供许多提高生产力的功能。

正如我上面提到的，Windows 管理器的选择完全是主观的，因此我建议您尝试所有这些并只使用适合您需要的那些。 选择窗口管理器时要考虑的最重要的事情是您的桌面环境和您的个人喜好。

就个人而言，我最喜欢的是 Qtile，因为它支持 python 编程语言以提供大量自定义选项。 但你最喜欢的可能是另一个。 所以，试试吧，找出你最喜欢的窗口管理器。
