原文:

Badal Aryal

Posted on Mar 4

Windows Manager is regarded as a system software and an efficient platform for Linux as it enables the user to control the placement and appearance of windows and its elements in a Graphic User Interface (GUI) as a windowing System. And it is a part of the desktop environment and improves our interaction with the system.

Finding the best windows manager is totally subjective and depends upon the needs of the user. Windows manager helps to resize and reposition your windows to organize the tools needed for certain tasks. It also steps up the multitasking potential by organizing the windows in a suitable manner with the help of keyboard shortcuts, mouse, or by automation scripts. It also makes the proper use of space on the screen.

Unlike a desktop environment window manager is just one piece of software. Generally speaking, there are two types of windows managers i.e tiling and stacking. Tiling refers to the process that tiles the window around each other and helps in avoiding white spaces whereas stacking refers to a process that stacks windows upon each other or is able to move them anywhere on the screen.

Window manager acts as a display server which is a bridge between Linux kernel and graphical interfaces like KDE plasma, Aqua, and Gnome Shell. Some of the common windows managers for Linux are Awesome, Compiz, Openbox, and i3.

Choosing the best windows manager depends upon individual preference but before knowing the window manager we need to learn about the different desktop environments that can be used in Linux. . However, some of the popular desktop environments are discussed below.

GNOME: GNOME stands for GNU Network Object Model Environment. Metacity is the default window manager for GNOME Environment but it is highly customizable. It supports Custom themes. It has a clean and intuitive interface and offers features like a virtual workspace and a built-in screen recorder. It is regarded as a tiling window manager for everyone.

KDE Plasma: KDE Plasma is one of the desktop Environments out there. It offers a customizable and visually appealing interface. It also provides a range of features like virtual desktops, widgets, and desktop effects. It can also be regarded as a full desktop environment with a window manager built into it.

Xfce: Xfce is a lightweight and fast desktop environment used in Linux. It is ideal for low-spec systems and offers a simple and interactive interface. It gives the desktop environment which also helps to get creative and productive at work. It is a free and open-source window manager that works with its own desktop environment.

Now, we knew about the popular desktop environment used in Linux, we can learn about some of the popular windows managers associated with them. Some of them are

1. Awesome
: Awesome is an open-source tiling window manager built using Lua programming language. Therefore, it is highly customizable for developers as well as users. It has evolved into a full-fledged Linux window manager. It offers simple and fast window management. . It also uses the XCM library instead of XLIB which helps to control latency in our system.

*2. DWM: * DWM stands for Dynamic Window Manager. It is simple yet effective having a minimal user interface. For this reason, it can be said to a better than other tiling window managers such as Awesome and xmonad. However, there is no configuration file, so it might be a little complicated for some users.

I3: i3 is a manual window manager that is of tiling type. It was built using the C programming language. It is highly customizable as it features different window organization settings to modify elements. It can be configured via plain text.

Xmonad:
Xmonad is a free, open-source dynamic tiling window manager for Linux. It was built using Haskell programming language. It can be personalized using the configuration file. It automates most of the window arrangements for the users.

IceWM:
IceWM is also a free stacking window manager for Linux. It was built using C++ programming language. It is suitable for lightweight machines as it is minimalistic and it also doesn’t compromise on essential features, usability, and customizability. It uses plain text to configure and customize the features.

SpectrWM: SpectrWM is a small, dynamic reparenting and tiling window manager built. It has features similar to Xmonad and DWM. Therefore, it is fast, concise, and compact. In it, we can edit and reload configuration when loading the program and can see the configuration files without logging out from the system.

Fluxbox: Fluxbox is a free and open-source window manager licensed under MIT. It is built using C++ programming language. It is lightweight and therefore it is fast and responsive. It is one of the best window managers because of the above-mentioned reasons.

HerbstluftWM: HerbstluftWM is another free and open-source window manager. It is highly configurable and uses Glib and Xlib. It is based on splitting frames into subframes which can be split further and filled with windows. Therefore, it makes good use of the screen.

Tmux: tmux is another free, open-source window manager. It is a terminal multiplexer where we can create multiple terminal sessions that we can access and control from a single screen. Here, we can also share our shell instance between different sessions for multiple use cases.

Qtile: Qtile is an open-source, fully featured, and hackable tiling window manager. It is built and configured using Python. It is a window manager that is simple to use, extensible, and highly customizable. The Qtile has a lot of features which include custom layouts, built-in commands, and widgets. In addition to these, here you can script it remotely to set up workspaces, manipulate windows or update status bar widgets.

Enlightenment:
Enlightenment is a window manager that provides an appealing, and modern design to the windows or display. The benefit of using this is that you don’t need to learn or know any programming language to set up the environment. It is also called as a desktop environment distinguished as a window manager.

JWM:
JWM stands for Joe’s Window Manager. It is an open-source lightweight window manager. It is built using c programming language and therefore, it is highly optimized to work well even on older or less powerful computers. By default, it can run on only the Xlib library but it can be customized to work with other libraries such as libXext for shape extension, libjpeg for JPEG backgrounds and icons, libpng PNG backgrounds and icons, Cairo and libRSVG for icons and backgrounds, and many more.

Sway:
Swayis a free, open-source, and lightweight tiling window manager. It is Wayland i3-compatible and automatically arranges application windows to maximize screen space. By default, it organizes windows into a grid. However, you can organize application windows vertically, horizontally, stacked, or tabbed. You can also change the size of windows or split windows into containers of several windows. Other features such as support for keyboard shortcuts, its usage of Wayland instead of Xorg, and gaps. It supports a majority of the commands included in Wayland i3.

Ratpoison: Ratpoison is a free, open-source lightweight Window Manager. The project was designed aims to build a simple program with minimal graphics and window decorations. Instead, it is modeled after the GNU Screen that gained its popularity in the virtual terminal community. Its advanced features include split windows that you can organize into non-overlapping frames. You can also maximize all windows within each frame. Moreover, Ratpoison is solely operated using keyboard commands.

Windows managers are as important as other system software for the smooth workflow and interaction with the system making you more productive. They can be a boon for those who interact with several apps and several display screens. But, we have to take into consideration of display environment before choosing the window manager. A majority of window manager offers similar features but the difference is only in the interaction with the user.

If you are getting started with using window manager, I recommend you the best choice would be i3 or xmonad as they are fast and minimal in design and offer a lot of features for productivity.

As I have mentioned above, the choice of windows manager is totally subjective so I recommend you try all of them and use only those that suit your needs. And the most important thing to consider while choosing a window manager is your Desktop Environment and your personal preferences.

Personally speaking, my favorite is Qtile as it supports python programming language for a lot of customization options. But your favorite might be another. So, try it and find out your favorite window manager.

Items.Title: 如何使用 TailwindCSS 构建完全响应式注册表单 | How to Build a Fully Responsive Sign Up Form Using TailwindCSS
Items.Description:
社区是具有地方、规范、宗教、价值观、习俗或身份等共性的社会单位。 有许多不同类型的社区，从宗教社区到社会社区，再到政治社区甚至技术社区。

作为技术人员或有抱负的技术人员，成为社区的一员非常重要，因为它可以帮助您成长并打开您的思路，让您了解解决特定问题的百万种不同可能性。

就我个人而言，我是许多不同社区的一员，其中之一是 icodethis，这个平台确实帮助我改进了 UI 设计并提高了我的编码技能。 你可以试一试，相信我你不会后悔的。

在许多情况下，我们以某种方式需要先注册才能充分使用某些东西。 它是一种离线服务，或者在线学习课程，甚至是新闻信件。 在我们的生活中，我们都至少注册过一次。

在本教程中，我们将构建这个很棒的组件。 注册表单可以有很多不同的形式。 所以我挑战你寻找其他设计，复制它，并在评论部分与我们分享。

目前，让我们使用这个，然后开始吧。

了解任务

将你的工作或设计分成不同的部分很重要，我总是这样做，它帮助我将它分解成更小的组件，我可以轻松构建，然后将它们连接在一起形成更大的组件。 本质上，我们有两个主要部分，为了便于参考，我们将其称为之前和之后 OR

代码结构

在设计组件时，我通常具有相同的结构。 我相信他们在某种程度上具有相同的根源。 这是怎么回事

<body> <!-- First Layer --> <div> <!-- Second Layer --> <div> <!-- Here Goes the content --> </div> </div> </body>

手术前

如前所述，我们将不同的部分称为“或之前”和“或之后”。

现在让我们来看看第一部分。

 <!-- Second Layer --> <div class="bg-white rounded-xl text-center px-14 sm:px-20 py-4 "> <!-- Form Header --> <div class="my-5 w-full [&>*]:mx-auto"> <h2 class="text-2xl w-4/5 font-semibold mb-3">Join the community</h2> <p class="text-sm text-center w-[97%]">Take your art to the next level. Get it seen by millions of people </p> </div> <!-- Join with Facebook --> <div class="bg-[#4968ad] text-white text-sm font-semibold shadow-sm shadow-[#4968ad] py-2 rounded cursor-pointer hover:scale-105"><h2>Join with Facebook</h2></div> . . . </div>

让我们理解上面的代码；

  • 对于表单头部，我们给出了一些基本属性，margin-inline( mx ) of auto，width full ( w-full ) 和 margin-block ( my )

  • 对于加入按钮，我们给它设置了背景颜色、阴影、白色文本和缩放悬停效果。

手术后

现在让我们在OR之后构建不同组件的不同部分

<!-- Second Layer --> <div class="bg-white rounded-xl text-center px-14 sm:px-20 py-4 "> . . . . . . . <!-- Input Fields --> <div class="grid grid-cols-2 [&>*]:border [&>*]:border-slate-300 [&>*]:mb-3 gap-2 [&>*]:rounded [&>*]:text-slate-600 [&>*]:w-full [&>*:focus]:ring-slate-900"> <input type="text" placeholder="First name" class="col-span-2 sm:col-span-1"> <input type="text" placeholder="Last name" class="col-span-2 sm:col-span-1"> <input type="email" placeholder="Email" class="col-span-2"> <input type="password" placeholder="Password" class="col-span-2"> </div> <!-- Footer--> <div> <div class="bg-[#1e2c4b] text-white text-sm font-semibold py-2 rounded text-center cursor-pointer shadow-sm shadow-[#1e2c4b] border border-[#1e2c4b] hover:bg-white hover:text-[#1e2c4b]"><h2>Create New Account</h2></div> <div class="[&>span]:text-[#4968ad] text-[0.7rem] my-5 [&>span]:cursor-pointer [&>span:hover]:underline w-[90%] mx-auto"> By joining you agree to our <span>Terms of Service</span> and <span>Privacy Policy</span> </div> </div> </div>

我想这很清楚，我们

  • 将输入字段与其他字段分开。 我们创建了一个包含两列的网格 grid grid-cols-2 为每个孩子添加了边框[&>*]:border [&>*]:border-slate-300 [&>*]:mb-3并且还给了每个孩子焦点效果[&>*:focus]:ring-slate-900 。

  • 这里的第二个子部分（页脚）有 2 部分号召性用语按钮，具有以下bg-[#1e2c4b] text-white text-sm font-semibold py-2 rounded text-center cursor-pointer shadow-sm shadow-[#1e2c4b] border border-[#1e2c4b] hover:bg-white hover:text-[#1e2c4b] . 这有点像 jagons。 但是让我们这样打破，我们应用深蓝色背景颜色，在悬停时变为白色。 我们为文本赋予了白色，在悬停时变为深蓝色。 当然，我们有相同颜色的边框和阴影。 希望它有意义。 应该就是这样！

但是等一下！ 我们做了 Before OR ✅ 和 After OR ✅，这是真的。 但是 OR 本身呢？ 让我们也完成它。 我们有一个普通的 Or 文本。

  • 至于Or文本左右两边的水平线，我们在左边的行使用伪::选择器before ，在右边的行使用after 。

    • 看起来很神奇，不是吗？😎

    结论

    我们刚刚构建了一个简单且响应迅速的注册表单组件，而无需打开我们的 CSS 文件😃。 感谢 Tailwindcss。

    许多雇主需要将此类组件添加到他们的网站，现在您应该感到自豪，因为您是少数知道如何在不到 5 分钟内构建它的人，您甚至可以在不离开 HTML 文档的情况下做到这一点 😎。

    您可以在Codepen上进行实时预览或在Github上查找代码

    还记得我们的挑战吗？ 检查注册表单的新设计，对其进行编码，并在评论部分与我们分享。

    如果您有任何担忧或建议，请不要犹豫，在评论部分留下！ 再见 ！ 👋 👋



    原文:

    A community is a social unit with commonality such as place, norms, religion, values, customs, or identity. There are many different type of communities, ranging from religions to social, to political and even Tech communities.

    Being part of a community as a tech person or aspiring tech individual, is very important as it helps you grow and also open your mind to a million different possibilities to solving a particular problem.

    I personally, I am part of many different communities, one of them is icodethis, a platform which really helped me improve my UI design and sharpen my coding skills. You can give it a try, and trust me you won’t regret.

    At many instances, we were in one way or the other required to sign up for something before being able to fully use it. It being an offline service, or to take a course online, or even to a news letter. We all sign up for something atleast once in our life.

    In this tutorial, we are going to build of this awesome component. A sign up form can have so many different forms. So I challenge you to look for other design, replicate it, and share it with us in the comment section.

    For the time being, Let’s use this one, and get started.

    Understanding the Task

    It’s important to divide you work or design into different parts, I always do that, it help me break it down to smaller components that I can easy build, then join them together to form the bigger component. (I call it Divide and Conquer 😉)

    Essentially, we have 2 main parts, for easy referencing, we will call it Before and After OR

    Structure of Code

    I generally have the same structure when it comes to designing components. I believe they somehow have the same root. 😃

    This is how it goes

    <body>
<!-- First Layer -->
 <div>
<!-- Second Layer -->
  <div>
        <!--   
              Here Goes the content
          -->
  </div>
 </div>

</body>

    Before OR

    As earlier agreed, we will reference the different parts as Before OR and After OR.

    Now Let’s have a look at the first part.

    <!-- Second Layer -->
<div class="bg-white rounded-xl text-center px-14 sm:px-20 py-4 ">
              <!-- Form Header -->
        <div class="my-5 w-full [&>*]:mx-auto">
            <h2 class="text-2xl w-4/5 font-semibold mb-3">Join the community</h2>
            <p class="text-sm text-center w-[97%]">Take your art to the next level. Get it 
                seen by millions of people
            </p>
        </div>
          <!-- Join with Facebook -->
        <div class="bg-[#4968ad] text-white text-sm font-semibold shadow-sm shadow-[#4968ad] py-2 rounded cursor-pointer hover:scale-105"><h2>Join with Facebook</h2></div>
          .
          .
          .
    </div>

    Let’s understand the above code;

    • bg stands for background, we gave a background of white to the container holding everything, give it a border radius (rounded-xl) and some padding-inline (px) and padding-block (py)

    • For the form header, we gave some basic properties, margin-inline(mx) of auto, width full (w-full) and margin-block (my)

    • For the join button, we gave it a background color, shadow, white text and a hover effect of scale.

    After OR

    Now let’s build the different pieces of the different components after OR

    <!-- Second Layer -->
<div class="bg-white rounded-xl text-center px-14 sm:px-20 py-4 ">
          .
          .
          .
          .
          .
          .
          .
      <!-- Input Fields -->
    <div class="grid grid-cols-2 [&>*]:border [&>*]:border-slate-300 [&>*]:mb-3 gap-2 [&>*]:rounded [&>*]:text-slate-600 [&>*]:w-full [&>*:focus]:ring-slate-900">
        <input type="text" placeholder="First name" class="col-span-2 sm:col-span-1">
        <input type="text" placeholder="Last name" class="col-span-2 sm:col-span-1">
        <input type="email" placeholder="Email" class="col-span-2">
        <input type="password" placeholder="Password" class="col-span-2">
    </div>


        <!-- Footer-->
    <div>
        <div class="bg-[#1e2c4b] text-white text-sm font-semibold py-2 rounded text-center cursor-pointer shadow-sm shadow-[#1e2c4b] border border-[#1e2c4b] hover:bg-white hover:text-[#1e2c4b]"><h2>Create New Account</h2></div>
        <div class="[&>span]:text-[#4968ad] text-[0.7rem] my-5 [&>span]:cursor-pointer [&>span:hover]:underline w-[90%] mx-auto">
            By joining you agree to our <span>Terms of Service</span>
            and <span>Privacy Policy</span>
        </div>
    </div>
</div>

    I guess this is pretty clear, we

    • Separate the input fields from the others. we create a grid with two columns grid grid-cols-2 added border to each child [&>*]:border [&>*]:border-slate-300 [&>*]:mb-3 and also gave each child a focus effect [&>*:focus]:ring-slate-900.

    • The second sub-part here (footer) has 2 part call to action button with the follow properties bg-[#1e2c4b] text-white text-sm font-semibold py-2 rounded text-center cursor-pointer shadow-sm shadow-[#1e2c4b] border border-[#1e2c4b] hover:bg-white hover:text-[#1e2c4b]. This kinda look like jagons. But let’s break as such, we apply a dark blue background color, which changes to white on hover. we gave a white color to text which changes to dark blue on hover. And of course, we have a border and shadow of same color. Hope it makes senses. 🤓
      And that should be it !

    But wait a minute ! We did Before OR ✅, and After OR ✅, that’s true. But what about OR itself ? 😮

    Let’s get that done too. . .✍️

    <!-- Second Layer -->
<div class="bg-white rounded-xl text-center px-14 sm:px-20 py-4 ">
          .
          .
          .
          .
          .
          .
          .
      <!-- Input Fields -->
<p class="text-slate-300 my-3 font-semibold relative before:content-[''] before:absolute before:w-[35%] before:bg-slate-300 before:h-0.5 before:left-4 before:rounded-full before:top-1/2 
after:content-[''] after:absolute after:w-[35%] after:bg-slate-300 after:h-0.5 after:right-4 after:rounded-full after:top-1/2">
    Or
</p>
          .
          .
          .
          .
          .

</div>

    • We have a normal Or text.

    • As for the horizontal line at the left and the right of the Or text, we are using the pseudo::selectors before for the line at the left and after for the line at the right.

    Looks amazing, isn’t it ?😎

    Conclusion

    We just built a simple and responsive Sign Up Form component without opening our CSS file😃. Thanks to Tailwindcss.

    Many employers will need such components to be added to their website, and right now you should be proud that you are one those few who knows how to build it in less than 5mins, and you can do that without even leaving you HTML document 😎.

    You can have a Live preview on Codepen or find the code on Github

    Remember our challenge ? Check on a new design of Sign Up Form, code it, and share it with us in the comment section.

    If you have any worries or suggestions, don’t hesitate to leave it in the comment section! 😊

    See ya ! 👋

Items.Title: MEP 预制图：5 大挑战和最佳实践 | MEP Prefabrication Drawings: Top 5 Challenges and Best Practices
Items.Description:
根据项目规模、地理位置和团队，MEP 工程师和承包商必须将 BIM 工作流与预制集成。 受到天气或运输挑战的项目可以在受控条件和清洁车间内利用异地制造。

使用预制组件可以提高施工效率。 然而，它需要基于模型的预制图纸和为制造商提供精确的 MEP 服务，以确保正确规划预制 MEP 组件。 如果没有一套正确的MEP 预制图纸，MEP 专业人员和承包商将很难跟上步伐。

制造 MEP 模块的制造商依赖于协调的 MEP 模块图纸、车间图纸和线轴图纸。 模块分为 3 种类型 - 水平吊顶、垂直立管和植物模块。

此外，预制机电图纸的设计对于复杂和大型建筑物或设施具有挑战性。

创建 MEP 制造图时面临的 5 大挑战

  • 结构和 MEP 数据不准确

记录错误的 MEP 模块结构要求（包括重量和负载计算）可能会给结构人员造成歧义。 预制图纸中不正确的尺寸或公差会导致 MEP 组件制造错误和现场冲突。

  • 一组错误的车间图纸或线轴图纸

项目工程师使用未经批准的 MEP 模块施工图会导致冲突、返工和项目延误。

  • 模块支持的模糊使用

不正确地使用模块支撑来连接和桥接机械系统的组件可能会导致设计和现场施工问题。

  • 支持系统设计不当

预制图纸中的重型支撑系统设计会导致更大的结构载荷和更高的材料浪费。

  • 缺少必要的支撑框架

缺少更小、更灵活的支撑框架、依赖的架空支撑和未连接的模块会导致制造和安装不准确。

无冲突和协调的图纸可以帮助客户节省 50% 的成本和 40% 的生产进度。

基于模型的预制图协调且无冲突。 制造商可以通过协调和无干扰的 MEP 预制图纸利用改进的预制洞察力。 这有助于制造商预制准确的 MEP 组件或设备，以实现快速、轻松的安装。

通过模型提取的预制图纸，MEP 设备的详细信息（例如名称、部件号、尺寸、位置等）的存在减少了代价高昂的返工。 从准确、完整和无冲突的预制图纸中获得的见解可帮助承包商最大限度地减少材料浪费并减少预算超支。

美国的一个住宅建筑项目需要 MEP 3D 模型和施工图作为业务需求。 协调且无冲突的 3D 模型帮助客户推动了 MEP 系统的完美制造和安装。 这减少了返工，从而节省了成本和时间。
基于 Revit 的 3D 模型在空间上是准确的。 根据准确的尺寸、规格和定制提取可预制的图纸，使预制过程更加简单和简化。

按照 MEP 预制图纸的最佳实践制造的预制模块尺寸准确。 这些模块可以在没有硬冲突或软冲突的情况下安装，从而减少现场中断。

成功实现机电预制的 5 大最佳实践

为 MEP 组件或设备提供精确的水平天花板空间可降低空间损失、快速简便的安装、隔音等。在支持行业指南的水平天花板中需要包含系统的 MEP 设备列表。

暴露或悬挂的水平天花板中 MEP 组件的准确清单可降低安装成本、提高能源效率、最大限度地减少 MEP 空间等。 制造商需要提取准确的线轴图纸以实现制造精度。

垂直立管主要分为管道、管道和导管。 基于基于模型的预制图纸在场外预制垂直立管可节省成本、提高现场审批、提高工人安全并提高施工效率。

带有机械、管道或电气组件的立管的 DFMA 图纸以及设备明细表、代码和焊接表示，以最大限度地提高安装效率。

立管模块需要在水平地面上显示，以便与不同楼层连接。

  • 在组装包括立管轴的墙壁之前，需要安装立管模块。

  • 预制图中的立管模块需要包括支架和吊耳。

    • 工厂模块需要准确建模，以便承包商提取准确的预制图纸。 模块化工厂或钢框架包括工艺设备、仪表、阀门、管道和电线。 工厂模块的 MEP 预制图包括工厂模块的每个组件或设备以及更改或变更、制造信息、尺寸和安装位置信息。

    生成准确和高质量的预制 MEP 图纸的最佳实践还包括聘请 BIM 技能组合和合格的绘图员来提供精确的绘图服务。 高质量的 MEP 预制图展示了零件制造或装配所需的每条信息。

    在实际制造或安装开始之前，需要包括精确的材料规格、公差、焊接位置、弯曲线、子组件、零件号、规格、角度、BOM 等。

    要制作出质量优良的机电预制图，需要各行业之间实现一定程度的协调。 与建筑师、制造商和结构工程师协调创建这些图纸支持快速反馈和解决提出的问题。

    马耳他的一家建筑公司将其对医疗保健建筑的要求外包给了 Hitech CADD Services。 以 2D MEP 图纸作为输入交付，MEP 协调图纸和 3D 模型是必需的可交付成果。 生成了 3D MEP 模型并提取了协调图，以帮助客户做出更好的决策并推动无障碍安装。
    开发 MEP 预制图的工作流程

    原理图或概念设计

    • 概念设计或示意图由 MEP 顾问根据建筑和结构布局为独特的行业开发。

    设计改进

    • 详细资料

      • 模型提取的图纸包含有关 MEP 组件尺寸、规格、组件、方向和位置的完整信息。

      协调且无冲突的模型

      • 通过集成各种 BIM 模型，生成具有高程的协调且无冲突的模型。

      图纸审查

      • 丰富协调图纸

        • 配合图纸用支撑架、转角、法兰进行加固，便于预制和安装。

        结论

        虽然模块化结构正在兴起，但预制结构对于预制 MEP 系统的安装至关重要。 通过准确、完整和高质量的预制 MEP CAD 图纸，可以在不中断的情况下安装 MEP 系统。

        从 3D BIM 模型生成详细图纸可确保承包商精确规划和执行 MEP 预制。 由专业BIM服务商外包无冲突预制图项目，确保在施工前阶段快速识别和解决MEP冲突。

        作者：

        哈里卡·辛格 (Harika Singh) 是一位院士和出版作家。 她对工程和技术的热情体现在她对技术趋势的深入报道中。 在印度和美国的知名机构工作 20 年，使她能够为业务利益相关者提供宝贵的见解，帮助他们通过数字化实现可扩展性和运营效率。



    原文:

    Depending on the project scale, geography and team, MEP engineers and contractors must integrate BIM workflows with prefabrication. Projects challenged by weather or transportation can leverage offsite fabrication under controlled conditions and clean workshops.

    Working with prefabricated components makes construction more efficient. However it requires model-based prefab drawings and precise MEP services for fabricators to ensure proper planning of prefab MEP components. Without the right set of MEP prefabrication drawings, it would be difficult for MEP professionals and contractors to keep pace.

    Flawless creation of MEP systems or modules requires a design that is feasible and accurate. Fabricators manufacturing MEP modules are dependent on coordinated MEP module drawings, shop drawings, and spool drawings. Modules are classified into 3 types - horizontal ceiling, vertical risers, and plant modules.

    Furthermore, the design of pre-fabricated MEP drawings is challenging for complex and large-scale buildings or facilities.

    Top 5 challenges faced while creating MEP fabrication drawings

    • Inaccurate structural and MEP data

    Logging wrong structural requirements for MEP modules including weight and load calculations can create ambiguities for structural personnel. Incorrect dimensions or tolerances in prefabrication drawings lead to faulty MEP component manufacturing and onsite clashes.

    • Wrongful set of shop drawings or spool drawings

    The use of unapproved shop drawings for MEP modules by project engineers leads to clashes, rework, and project delays.

    • Ambiguous use of module supports

    Inaccurate use of module supports for components to connect and bridge mechanical systems can lead to design and onsite construction problems.

    • Improper design of support systems

    Heavy support system design in prefabrication drawings leads to greater structure loads and higher material wastage.

    • Lack of required support frames

    The absence of smaller and flexible supporting frames, dependent overhead support, and unlinked modules lead to inaccurate fabrication and installation.

    Clash-free and coordinated drawings can help clients gain 50% in cost savings and 40% in production schedules.

    Top 5 benefits of MEP prefabrication drawings for construction workflows

    1. Deviation tracking with agile comparisons

    Model-based prefabrication drawings are coordinated and clash-free. Agile comparisons between various data points like 3D models and drawings help fabricators and contractors to derive comparative results for greater constructability.

    2. Greater prefabrication insights through streamlined drawing interpretation

    Fabricators can leverage improved prefabrication insights through coordinated and interference-free MEP prefabrication drawings. This helps manufacturers prefabricate accurate MEP components or equipment for quick and hassle-free installation.

    3. Accurate and detailed installation information to reduce rework and budget overruns

    The existence of detailed information for MEP equipment such as name, part number, dimensions, location, etc. through model-extracted prefabrication drawings reduces costly rework. Insights derived from accurate, complete, and clash-free prefabrication drawings assist contractors to minimize material waste and diminish budget overruns.

    A residential building construction project in the US required an MEP 3D model and construction drawings as a business need. The team at Hitech CADD Services created a 3D MEP model at LOD 400 with extracted construction drawings. The coordinated and clash-free 3D model helped the client drive flawless fabrication and installation of MEP systems. This reduced rework, leading to cost and time savings.
    Read the case study >>

    4. Accurate geometry based on dimensions, specs, and customization for precise prefabrication

    Revit-based 3D models are spatially accurate. The extraction of prefabrication-ready drawings based on accurate dimensions, specs, and customization makes the prefabrication process easier and streamlined.

    5. High-quality modules create minimal installation disruption onsite

    Prefabricated modules manufactured following best practices for MEP prefabrication drawings are dimensionally accurate. These modules can be installed without hard or soft clashes which leads to diminished onsite disruption.

    Top 5 best practices to achieve successful MEP prefabrication

    1. Accurate and detailed MEP information for Horizontal Ceiling

    Providing precise horizontal ceiling space for MEP components or equipment drives lower space loss, quick and easy installation, soundproofing, etc. A systematic list of MEP equipment needs to be included in the horizontal ceiling that are supported industry guidelines.

    An accurate checklist of MEP components in an exposed or suspended horizontal ceiling reduces installation costs, increase energy efficiency, minimize MEP space, and more. Accurate spool drawings need to be extracted for fabricators to achieve manufacturing precision.

    2. Flawless MEP prefabrication drawings for Vertical Risers

    Vertical risers are predominantly categorized as ducts, pipes, and conduits. MEP contractors must make sure these components are fabricated accurately for unhindered installation. Prefabrication of vertical risers offsite based on model-based prefabrication drawings saves costs, boosts site approvals, improves worker safety, and improves construction efficiency.

    DFMA drawings of risers with mechanical, pipework, or electrical components with equipment schedules, codes, and weld representations to maximize installation effectiveness.

    Following best practices to follow for vertical risers in MEP prefabrication drawings include:

    • Riser modules need to be shown at a horizontal ground level to be connected with various floors.

    • Riser modules need to be installed before assembling walls that include riser shafts.

    • Riser modules in prefabrication drawings need to include brackets and lifting lugs.

    3. Precise prefabrication drawings of Plant Modules for pre-assembly, pre-wiring, and installation

    Plant modules need to be modeled accurately for contractors to extract accurate prefabrication drawings. Modular plants or steel frameworks include process equipment, instrumentation, valves, piping, and electrical wiring. Prefabrication drawings for MEP plant modules reduce safety concerns during construction and simplify component modification.

    MEP prefabrication drawings for plant modules include every component or equipment for plant modules along with changes or alterations, fabrication information, dimensions, and placement information for installation.

    4. BIM skillsets and qualified drafting

    Best practices to generate accurate and high-quality prefabrication MEP drawings also include hiring BIM skillsets and qualified drafters for precise drawing services. Good quality MEP prefabrication drawings showcase every piece of information that is needed for part manufacturing or assembly.

    Precise material specifications, tolerances, weld locations, bend lines, sub-assemblies, part numbers, specifications, angles, BOMs, etc. need to be included before actual fabrication or installation begins.

    5. Communication and collaboration between designers, contractors, and fabricators

    To produce excellent quality MEP prefabrication drawings, a certain degree of coordination needs to be achieved between various trades. Coordinating with architects, manufacturers, and structural engineers to create these drawings supports quick feedback and solutions for raised queries.

    A construction company from Malta outsourced its requirements for healthcare construction to Hitech CADD Services. With 2D MEP drawings delivered as input, MEP coordination drawings and 3D models were required deliverables. 3D MEP models were generated and coordination drawings were extracted to help client make better decisions and drive hassle-free installation.
    Read the case study >>

    Workflow to develop MEP prefabrication drawings

    Schematic or conceptual design

    • Conceptual designs or schematics are developed by MEP consultants for unique trades based on architecture and structure layout.

    Design enhancements

    • MEP contractors and prefabricators further enhance the design through clash-free and coordinated models.

    Detailed information

    • Model-extracted drawings contain complete information on MEP component dimensions, specs, assemblies, orientation, and location.

    Coordinated and clash-free model

    • A coordinated and clash-free model with elevations is generated by integrating various BIM models.

    Drawing review

    • Review of MEP coordinated drawings by MEP contractors and consultants.

    Enrichment of coordinated drawings

    • Coordinated drawings are reinforced with supporting frames, corners, and flanges for prefabrication and installation.

    Conclusion

    While modular construction is on the rise, Pre-fabrication construction is crucial for the installation of prefabricated MEP systems. MEP systems can be installed without any interruptions through accurate, complete, and high-quality prefabricated MEP CAD drawings.

    Generating detailed drawings from 3D BIM models ensure precise planning and execution for contractors for MEP prefabrication. Outsourcing projects for clash-free prefabrication drawings by a professional BIM service provider ensures quick identification and resolution of MEP conflicts in the preconstruction stage.

    Authored by:

    Harika Singh is an academician and published writer. Her passion for engineering and technology reflects in the in-depth coverage she provides on technology trends. 20 years of work association with institutes of repute across India and the US positions her to provide valuable insights to business stakeholders on achieving scalability and operational efficiencies through digitalization.

为什么 Appium 是最好的？

随着 Appium 在企业移动性中引起轰动，移动团队仍在寻找利用 Appium 成功自动化测试的方法。 现在让我们看看 Appium 成为移动自动化工具最佳选择的主要因素：

使用标准 API：Appium 之所以广受欢迎，是因为它在所有平台上都使用标准API ，因此无需修改代码或重新编译您的应用程序。 但是，仍然需要单独的 iOS 和 Android 脚本，因为两个平台上的 UI 元素各不相同。

使用任何与 WebDriver 兼容的语言： Appium使您可以自由地编写和运行测试，而不必被锁定在特定的语言或框架中。 任何与 WebDriver 兼容的语言，如带有 Selenium WebDriver API 的 Perl、Java、PHP、C#、Python、Ruby、带有 Node.js 的 Javascript 都可以用于编写测试。

选择的测试框架：Appium 为移动团队提供了使用他们选择的测试框架的灵活性。 早些时候，测试只能使用 Apple 库的 UI Automation 通过 Javascript 编写，或者基于 Java 的测试只能通过 Google 的 UI Automator 编写。 跨平台测试自动化：具有在 Android 和 iOS 设备上进行测试的能力使其成为最好的跨平台移动应用程序测试自动化工具。 为了使用 Selenium WebDriver 与 Android 和 iOS 交互，Appium 使用 JSON 有线协议。 在 v9.3 之后的较新版本的 iOS 中，Instruments api 已被弃用，现在使用 XCUITest 框架。
该方法在 Android 中也类似，Appium 将自动化命令代理到设备上运行的 UIAutomator 测试用例。 开源：作为开源测试框架是 Appium 的最大优势之一，因为它支持 Simulators、 Emulators 、真实设备，当然还有 iOS 和 Android 的本机、混合和 Web 应用程序测试。 您可以在云保留的会话中为任何 iOS 或 Android 设备在本地启动从 Appium 库创建的测试脚本。 它还支持 iOS 上的 Safari 和 Chrome Mozilla 或 Android 上的任何内置“浏览器”应用程序。
有许多用于移动应用程序测试的自动化工具。 测试人员通常选择 Appium 作为最好的移动测试工具。 移动自动化测试工具的比较可以在语言支持和持续集成的基础上进行。 用于移动应用程序测试的最常见的自动化测试工具是 Appium、Robotium 和 Calabash。 如果您正在寻找 iOS 应用程序自动化测试工具，那么 Appium 和 Calabash 可以为您完成这项工作。

下面是表格展示，让您了解 Appium 与不同功能和工具的兼容性。

为何内置 Appium 支持的移动设备云？

开始使用自动化或考虑将 Appium 作为一种选择的团队必须探索一种替代方案，以使用内置 Appium 的移动设备云。

移动设备云不仅有助于管理和共享设备，还有助于简化自动化测试和持续交付流程。 内置 Appium 的移动设备云使团队可以轻松开始自动化并在以后进行扩展。 此外，它将为使用开源平台进行测试时可能出现的任何障碍提供额外的缓冲。 让我们来看看在移动设备云上拥有内置 Appium 支持的一些好处：

支持多设备并行测试

原文:

Why Appium is The Best?

With Appium creating a buzz in enterprise mobility, mobility teams are still finding ways to successfully automate tests leveraging Appium. Appium being an open source tool is the perfect choice for automating native, mobile web and hybrid applications on their respective platforms.

Let us now see the major factors behind Appium being the best choice for mobile automation tool:

An impressive expression by Appium.io showing why to choose Appium

Use of standard API: Appium is widely popular because modification of codes or a recompilation of your app is not required as it uses the standard API in all the platforms. Appium makes it effortless to create your tests against iOS and Android platforms with same API. But, separate iOS and Android scripts are still needed as the UI elements vary on both the platforms.

Use any WebDriver compatible language: Appium gives the freedom from getting locked into a particular language or framework to write and run the tests. Any WebDriver compatible language like Perl with Selenium WebDriver API, Java, PHP, C#, Python, Ruby, Javascript with Node.js can be used for writing the tests.

Testing Framework of Choice: Appium gives flexibility to mobility teams to use testing framework of their choice. Earlier, tests could only be written through Javascript using the UI Automation of library for Apple or Java based tests could only be written through UI Automator of Google. Appium completely changed this scenario.

Cross-platform test automation: Having the capabilities to test on both Android and iOS devices makes it the best cross-platform mobile app test automation tool. In order to interact with Android and iOS with Selenium WebDriver, Appium uses the JSON wire protocol. Appium makes use of the libraries provided by Apple with the help of an application called Instruments to automate iOS apps. In newer versions of iOS after v9.3, the Instruments api has been deprecated and now use XCUITest framework.
The method is similar in Android also where Appium proxies the automation command to the UIAutomator test case running on the device. Android has a native UI automation framework called UIAutomator which supports running JUnit test cases from the command line directly into the device.

Open Source: Being an open source testing framework is one of the biggest advantages of Appium as it supports Simulators, Emulators, real devices, and of course, native, hybrid and web application testing of iOS and Android. Appium having a large and thriving open community makes it easier for new automation engineers to clarify their doubts.

You can instigate test scripts created from Appium libraries locally, on a session reserved by the Cloud, for any iOS or Android device. Appium integrates with continuous integration servers to ensure better results and drives GUI-related widgets and controls, allowing the same scripts to run for different software versions of various apps. Appium can automate native, web and hybrid mobile apps, and you can test on a real device, a simulator, or an emulator. It also supports Safari on iOS and Chrome Mozilla or any built-in ‘Browser’ app on Android.
There are many automation tools for mobile application testing. Testers usually choose Appium as the best mobile testing tool. Mobile automation testing tools comparison can be done on the basis of language support and continuous integration. The most common automation testing tools used for mobile application testing are Appium, Robotium, and Calabash. If you are looking for iOS app automation testing tools then Appium and Calabash can do the job for you.

Here is tabular representation for you to understand Appium’s compatibility with different features and tools.

Why mobile device cloud with built-in Appium support?

Teams who are getting started with Automation or are considering Appium as an option, must explore an alternative to go for a mobile device cloud with built-in Appium.

A mobile device cloud not only assists in managing and sharing devices, but also helps in streamlining automated testing and continuous delivery processes. A mobile device cloud with built-in Appium makes it easy for teams to get started with automation and scale up later. Furthermore, it will give additional cushion against any sort of roadblock that might occur while using an open-source platform for tests. Let’s have a look at some of the benefits of having a built-in Appium support on a mobile device cloud:

Supports parallel testing on multiple devices

Reduces the complexity of test frameworks

Creation of appium scripts become easy

Streamlines the CI/CD process

At pCloudy, we are dedicated towards helping enterprise mobility teams make the process of mobile development, testing and device sharing seamless and faster by integrating it with cutting edge tools like Appium. Read this blog to get a comprehensive analysis sheet to quickly choose which open-source test automation tool will be right for your automation testing.

Sign up on pCloudy and automate your tests using best open source automation tool for faster and better delivery of apps.

Items.Title: 了解功能测试和单元测试之间的差异 | Understanding the Differences Between Functional Testing and Unit Testing
Items.Description:
功能测试和单元测试是应用程序开发中必不可少的测试方法。 在这篇博文中，我们将深入分析两种测试类型之间的差异、何时使用每种类型以及它们识别出的错误类型。 我们还将研究如何在两种测试方法之间取得平衡，从而交付高质量的产品。

功能测试

功能测试是一种重要的测试方法，它专注于应用程序的基本功能，以验证它是否按照业务要求运行。 在创建功能测试用例之前，需要透彻理解应用程序的操作需求。

功能测试评估应用程序的页面转换、用户界面、数据存储、屏幕切换能力和整体性能，侧重于用户的观点。 它确保应用程序的可访问性、可用性和整体用户体验，确保应用程序满足其目标用户的期望。

开发功能测试用例

要进行有效的功能测试，QA 专业人员必须彻底审查业务需求文档，熟悉应用程序，并为将要评估的特定功能设计测试策略。 他们还应该为所需的功能测试选择最佳工具，并将实际结果与预期结果进行比较以确定测试是否成功。

单元测试

单元测试允许对应用程序的各种组件进行单独测试。 每个组件，包括文本框、按钮和文本显示等元素，都经过审查以确保其按预期运行。 术语“单元测试”源自将这些组件视为不同“单元”的做法。 即使是最小的应用程序组件也会经历此测试过程。

在单元测试期间，每个组件都被单独评估以识别任何潜在问题。 但是，与外部实体（例如外部数据库、网络或文件）交互的组件不受单元测试的约束。 开发人员从开发的早期阶段就开始运行单元测试，以便在开发之前发现任何问题。

开发单元测试用例

单元测试涉及通过专用测试用例评估应用程序的每个部分。 每个测试用例对应一个特定的按钮按下或文本框条目，确保页面中所有元素的功能都经过全面测试。 为应用程序的每个页面创建多个单元测试用例，以确保不会遗漏任何元素。

由于有多种可用的单元测试框架，因此必须选择最适合开发人员编程语言的一种。 通过利用正确的框架，开发人员可以确保他们的单元测试有效且可靠。

功能测试与单元测试比较

功能测试

单元测试

它评估应用程序的可用性、可访问性和流程。

分别评估每个组件的操作。

包括多种测试方法，包括单元测试和系统测试。

它是功能测试的一个子集

需要更长的时间并检查应用程序的整体可用性。

它相对较短且效率更高，因为它专注于特定组件。

查找一般问题而不是具体问题。

它揭示了具体问题。

旨在验证软件的功能、可靠性和准确性，以确保它满足最终用户的需求。

旨在确保应用程序的各个单元或组件的功能，以在开发过程的早期发现缺陷并提高代码质量。

假设我们有一个允许用户预订拼车服务的移动应用程序。 为了测试应用程序的功能，我们可能会进行测试以确保用户可以完成以下任务：创建帐户登录应用程序搜索拼车选项选择行程并设置上车和下车地点查看预计价格和到达时间确认预订跟踪行程并接收实时更新评价行程并提供反馈

这些测试将模拟与应用程序的真实交互和场景，并确保应用程序按预期运行。

另一方面，单元测试将专注于应用程序的各个组件。 例如，我们可能会测试以下功能： 用户身份验证：用户是否可以创建帐户并登录？ 密码是否已加密并安全存储？搜索功能：应用程序是否会根据用户输入返回准确的搜索结果？ 搜索游乐设施时是否存在任何性能问题？预订流程：用户是否可以选择游乐设施并确认预订？ 预计价格和到达时间是否正确显示？乘车跟踪：该应用程序是否提供乘车位置和预计到达时间的实时更新？ 地图或 GPS 功能是否有任何问题？这些测试将有助于识别应用程序特定组件中的任何错误或问题，并确保每个单元都按预期工作。

通过进行功能测试和单元测试，我们可以确保移动应用程序正常运行并提供积极的用户体验。

对于一般功能测试，使用功能测试框架。

结合单元测试框架

功能测试和单元测试的目标是什么？

功能测试

功能测试旨在验证应用程序的可靠性、准确性和整体功能，以确保它满足最终用户的需求。 它涉及执行一系列测试，以复制真实场景和与应用程序的交互，以将结果与预期结果进行比较。 功能测试的主要目标是确保应用程序按预期运行并且所有组件都可以互操作。 功能测试对于识别可能对用户体验产生负面影响的任何问题至关重要。

单元测试

单元测试用于在开发过程的早期识别和修复缺陷。 单元测试的主要目标是提高代码质量并防止回归问题。 通过分别测试应用程序的每个组件，可以更轻松地确定问题的根本原因并快速解决问题。 此外，单元测试用作文档，清楚地概述了系统的用途

结论

了解单元测试和功能测试之间的区别对于测试人员和开发人员来说很重要。 虽然这两种测试方法都很关键，但它们有不同的用途，不应互换使用。 功能测试更为广泛和耗时，但它验证系统是否按照业务要求运行。

为了交付高质量的产品，在单元测试和功能测试之间取得适当的平衡至关重要，确保两种测试方法都得到适当执行。

原文:

Functional testing and unit testing are essential testing approaches in application development. In this blog post, we will provide an in-depth analysis of differences between the two types of testing, when to use each type, and the types of errors they identify. We’ll also look at how achieving a balance between the two testing approaches will result in the delivery of a high-quality product.

Functional Testing

Functional testing is a crucial testing methodology that concentrates on an application’s essential features to verify that it operates in accordance with business requirements. Prior to creating functional test cases, a thorough comprehension of the application’s operational needs is required.

Functional testing assesses an application’s page transitions, user interface, data storage, screen-switching capabilities, and overall performance, focusing on the user’s viewpoint. It ensures the application’s accessibility, usability, and overall user experience, making sure that the application meets the desired expectations of its intended users.

Developing Functional Test Cases

To conduct effective functional testing, a QA professional must thoroughly review the business requirements document, familiarize themselves with the application, and design a test strategy for the specific functionality that will be evaluated. They should also select the best tool for the functional testing required and compare actual results with expected results to determine the test’s success.

Unit testing

Unit testing allows for the individual testing of an application’s various components. Each component, including elements like text boxes, buttons, and text displays, undergoes scrutiny to ensure it performs as intended. The term “unit testing” derives from the practice of viewing these components as distinct “units”. Even the smallest app component undergoes this testing process.

During unit testing, each component is assessed separately to identify any potential issues. However, components that interface with external entities, such as external databases, networks, or files, are not subject to unit testing. Developers begin running unit tests from the early stages of development to catch any issues before they can progress.

Developing Unit Test Cases

Unit testing involves evaluating every part of an application through dedicated test cases. Each test case corresponds to a specific button press or text box entry, ensuring that the functionality of all elements in a page is thoroughly tested. Multiple unit test cases are created for each page of the application, to guarantee that no element is missed.

As there are several unit testing frameworks available, it’s essential to select the most suitable one for the developer’s programming language. By leveraging the right framework, developers can ensure that their unit tests are effective and reliable.

Functional Test vs. Unit Test Comparison

Functional Testing

Unit Testing

It evaluates the usability, accessibility, and flow of the application.

Evaluates the operation of each component separately.

Includes a variety of testing methods, including unit testing and system testing.

It’s a subset of functional testing

Takes longer and examines an application’s entire usability.

It is relatively shorter and more efficient as it concentrates on specific components.

Find generic problems without being specific.

It reveals specific problems.

Aims to verify the software’s functionality, dependability, and accuracy to ensure it satisfies end user needs.

Aims to ensure the functionality of individual units or components of an application to find defects early in the development process and enhance code quality.

Let’s say we have a mobile app that allows users to book a ride-sharing service. To test the app’s functionality, we might conduct tests to make sure that users can complete the following tasks: Create an accountLogin to the appSearch for ride-sharing optionsChoose a ride and set the pickup and drop-off locationsReview the estimated price and time of arrivalConfirm the bookingTrack the ride and receive real-time updatesRate the ride and provide feedback

These tests would mimic real-world interactions and scenarios with the application, and ensure that the app is working as intended.

Unit testing, on the other hand, would focus on individual components of the app. For example, we might test the following functions: User authentication: Can users create an account and log in? Are passwords encrypted and securely stored?Search functionality: Does the app return accurate search results based on user input? Are there any performance issues when searching for rides?Booking process: Are users able to select a ride and confirm the booking? Is the estimated price and time of arrival displayed correctly?Ride tracking: Does the app provide real-time updates on the ride’s location and ETA? Are there any issues with the map or GPS functionality?These tests would help identify any bugs or issues in specific components of the app, and ensure that each unit is working as intended.

By conducting both functional and unit tests, we can ensure that the mobile app is functioning correctly and providing a positive user experience.

For general functional testing, functional testing frameworks are employed.

Frameworks for unit testing are combined

What Do Functional Testing and Unit Testing Aim to Achieve?

Functional Testing

Functional testing aims to verify the applications dependability, accuracy, and overall functionality to ensure that it meets the end-users’ needs. It involves performing a series of tests that replicate real-world scenarios and interactions with the application to compare the results with the expected outcomes. The primary goal of functional testing is to ensure that the application operates as intended and that all components are interoperable. Functional testing is vital in identifying any issues that could negatively impact the user experience.

Unit Testing

Unit testing is used to identify and fix defects early in the development process. The primary objective of unit testing is to improve code quality and prevent regression issues. By testing each component of the application separately, it becomes easier to identify the root cause of problems and resolve them quickly. Additionally, unit tests serve as documentation, clearly outlining how the system is intended

Conclusion

It is important for testers and developers to understand the difference between unit testing and functional testing. While both testing methods are critical, they serve different purposes and should not be used interchangeably. Functional testing is more extensive and time-consuming, but it verifies that the system operates according to the business requirements.

To deliver a high-quality product, it is crucial to strike the right balance between unit testing and functional testing, ensuring that both testing methods are appropriately executed.

Items.Title: 从 C 代码到机器代码：了解编译过程 | From C Code to Machine Code: Understanding the Compilation Process
Items.Description:
编译过程涉及几个阶段：

  1. 预处理
  2. 解析
  3. 优化
  4. 代码生成

编译过程的每个阶段都执行特定的任务，并可能以各种方式修改代码。 让我们通过一些示例更详细地了解每个阶段。

第一阶段：预处理

编译的第一阶段是预处理，预处理器获取 C 源代码并应用任何预处理器指令。 这些指令以 # 符号开头，用于执行各种任务，例如包含头文件、定义宏或条件编译。

下面是一个使用预处理器指令的 C 源文件示例：

 #include <stdio.h> #define PI 3.14159 int main() { float radius = 5.0; float area = PI * radius * radius; printf("The area of a circle with radius %f is %f\n", radius, area); return 0; }

在这个例子中，我们使用 #include 指令来包含标准输入/输出头文件，它提供了类似 printf 的函数，用于将输出打印到控制台。 我们还定义了一个名为 PI 的宏，稍后用于计算圆的面积。

预处理器获取此源代码并将其扩展为编译器可以使用的形式。 生成的代码可能如下所示：

 // The contents of stdio.h are included here // ... // The value of PI is defined as 3.14159 #define PI 3.14159 int main() { float radius = 5.0; float area = 3.14159 * radius * radius; printf("The area of a circle with radius %f is %f\n", radius, area); return 0; }

如您所见，预处理器已将 #include 指令替换为 stdio.h 头文件的内容，并将对 PI 的引用替换为其实际值。

第二阶段：解析

编译的下一阶段是解析，编译器根据 C 语言的规则获取预处理代码并分析其结构。 解析器检查语法错误、语义错误和其他可能阻止代码正确运行的问题。

下面是一个包含一些语法错误的 C 源文件示例：

 #include <stdio.h> int main() { int x = 5; if (x > 10) { printf("x is greater than 10\n"); else { printf("x is less than or equal to 10\n"); } return 0; }

在这个例子中，我们忘记了在 if 语句中关闭条件后面的括号，也忘记了关闭 else 语句后面的大括号。

当编译器尝试解析此代码时，它会遇到这些错误并生成如下错误消息：

 example.c: In function 'main': example.c:6:5: error: expected ')' before '{' token if (x > 10) { ^ example.c:9:5: error: expected '}' before 'else' else { ^

如您所见，编译器已检测到语法错误并指出它们在代码中的位置。优化以提高其性能。 这些优化可以包括删除未使用的代码、重新排序指令以获得更好的缓存性能以及简化复杂的表达式。

下面是一个可以优化的 C 源文件示例：

 #include <stdio.h> int main() { int x = 5; int y = 10; int z = (x * y) / 2; printf("The value of z is %d\n", z); return 0; }

在此示例中，我们通过将 x 和 y 相乘然后除以 2 来计算 z 的值。编译器可以通过在编译时而非运行时执行乘法和除法来优化此代码，从而产生更快的代码。

优化后的代码可能看起来像这样：

 #include <stdio.h> int main() { int z = 25; printf("The value of z is %d\n", z); return 0; }

阶段 4：代码生成

编译的最后阶段是代码生成，编译器在此阶段采用优化代码并生成机器可读的目标代码或可执行文件。 这涉及将 C 代码翻译成特定于目标平台的汇编语言或机器代码。

以下是已编译并生成为可执行文件的 C 源文件示例：

 #include <stdio.h> int main() { printf("Hello, Giganoto!\n"); return 0; }

编译和链接此代码时，它可能会生成如下可执行文件：

 .file "example.c" .section .rodata .LC0: .string "Hello, Giganoto!" .text .globl main .type main, @function main: pushq %rbp movq %rsp, %rbp movl $.LC0, %edi call puts movl $0, %eax popq %rbp ret

如您所见，生成的代码采用汇编语言格式，并包含打印“Hello, world!”的指令。 消息到控制台。

结论

总之，C 编译器获取人类可读的 C 源代码并将其转换为机器可读的目标代码或可执行文件。 编译过程涉及几个阶段，包括预处理、解析、优化和代码生成。 在每个阶段，都可以通过各种方式修改代码以提高其性能、优化其结构或消除错误。

了解编译过程以及代码在此过程中发生的更改可以帮助您编写更高效且无错误的 C 程序。 通过学习编译过程的基础知识，您可以更深入地了解代码的工作原理以及如何对其进行优化以获得更好的性能。



原文:

A C compiler is a program that translates human-readable C source code into machine-readable object code or executable files. The process of compilation involves several stages:

  1. Preprocessing
  2. Parsing
  3. Optimization
  4. Code generation

Each stage of the compilation process performs specific tasks and may modify the code in various ways. Let's take a look at each stage in more detail, along with some examples.

Stage 1: Preprocessing

The first stage of compilation is preprocessing, where the preprocessor takes the C source code and applies any preprocessor directives. These directives start with a # symbol and are used to perform various tasks, such as including header files, defining macros, or conditional compilation.

Here's an example of a C source file that uses preprocessor directives:

#include <stdio.h>
#define PI 3.14159

int main() {
    float radius = 5.0;
    float area = PI * radius * radius;

    printf("The area of a circle with radius %f is %f\n", radius, area);

    return 0;
}

In this example, we use the #include directive to include the standard input/output header file, which provides functions like printf for printing output to the console. We also define a macro called PI, which is later used in the calculation of the area of a circle.

The preprocessor takes this source code and expands it into a form that the compiler can work with. The resulting code might look something like this:

// The contents of stdio.h are included here
// ...

// The value of PI is defined as 3.14159
#define PI 3.14159

int main() {
    float radius = 5.0;
    float area = 3.14159 * radius * radius;

    printf("The area of a circle with radius %f is %f\n", radius, area);

    return 0;
}

As you can see, the preprocessor has replaced the #include directive with the contents of the stdio.h header file and replaced the reference to PI with its actual value.

Stage 2: Parsing

The next stage of compilation is parsing, where the compiler takes the preprocessed code and analyzes its structure according to the rules of the C language. The parser checks for syntax errors, semantic errors, and other problems that may prevent the code from running correctly.

Here's an example of a C source file that contains some syntax errors:

#include <stdio.h>

int main() {
    int x = 5;
    if (x > 10) {
        printf("x is greater than 10\n");
    else {
        printf("x is less than or equal to 10\n");
    }

    return 0;
}

In this example, we forgot to close the parentheses after the condition in the if statement, and we forgot to close the curly brace after the else statement.

When the compiler tries to parse this code, it will encounter these errors and generate error messages like:

example.c: In function ‘main’:
example.c:6:5: error: expected ‘)’ before ‘{’ token
     if (x > 10) {
     ^
example.c:9:5: error: expected ‘}’ before ‘else’
     else {
     ^

As you can see, the compiler has detected the syntax errors and pointed out their locations in the code.optimizations to improve its performance. These optimizations can include removing unused code, reordering instructions for better cache performance, and simplifying complex expressions.

Here's an example of a C source file that could be optimized:

#include <stdio.h>

int main() {
    int x = 5;
    int y = 10;
    int z = (x * y) / 2;

    printf("The value of z is %d\n", z);

    return 0;
}

In this example, we calculate the value of z by multiplying x and y and then dividing by 2. The compiler could optimize this code by performing the multiplication and division at compile-time instead of at runtime, resulting in faster code.

The optimized code might look something like this:

#include <stdio.h>

int main() {
    int z = 25;

    printf("The value of z is %d\n", z);

    return 0;
}

Stage 4: Code Generation

The final stage of compilation is code generation, where the compiler takes the optimized code and generates machine-readable object code or executable files. This involves translating the C code into assembly language or machine code, which is specific to the target platform.

Here's an example of a C source file that has been compiled and generated into an executable file:

#include <stdio.h>

int main() {
    printf("Hello, Giganoto!\n");

    return 0;
}

When this code is compiled and linked, it might generate an executable file like this:

.file   "example.c"
    .section    .rodata
.LC0:
    .string "Hello, Giganoto!"
    .text
    .globl  main
    .type   main, @function
main:
    pushq   %rbp
    movq    %rsp, %rbp
    movl    $.LC0, %edi
    call    puts
    movl    $0, %eax
    popq    %rbp
    ret

As you can see, the generated code is in assembly language format and includes instructions for printing the "Hello, world!" message to the console.

Conclusion

In conclusion, a C compiler takes human-readable C source code and transforms it into machine-readable object code or executable files. The compilation process involves several stages, including preprocessing, parsing, optimization, and code generation. At each stage, the code may be modified in various ways to improve its performance, optimize its structure, or eliminate errors.

Understanding the process of compilation and the changes that occur to your code along the way can help you write more efficient and error-free C programs. By learning the basics of the compilation process, you can gain a deeper understanding of how your code works and how to optimize it for better performance.

Items.Title: 了解功能测试和单元测试之间的差异 | Understanding the Differences Between Functional Testing and Unit Testing
Items.Description:
功能测试和单元测试是应用程序开发中必不可少的测试方法。 在这篇博文中，我们将深入分析两种测试类型之间的差异、何时使用每种类型以及它们识别出的错误类型。 我们还将研究如何在两种测试方法之间取得平衡，从而交付高质量的产品。

功能测试

功能测试是一种重要的测试方法，它专注于应用程序的基本功能，以验证它是否按照业务要求运行。 在创建功能测试用例之前，需要透彻理解应用程序的操作需求。

功能测试评估应用程序的页面转换、用户界面、数据存储、屏幕切换能力和整体性能，侧重于用户的观点。 它确保应用程序的可访问性、可用性和整体用户体验，确保应用程序满足其目标用户的期望。

开发功能测试用例

要进行有效的功能测试，QA 专业人员必须彻底审查业务需求文档，熟悉应用程序，并为将要评估的特定功能设计测试策略。 他们还应该为所需的功能测试选择最佳工具，并将实际结果与预期结果进行比较以确定测试是否成功。

单元测试

单元测试允许对应用程序的各种组件进行单独测试。 每个组件，包括文本框、按钮和文本显示等元素，都经过审查以确保其按预期运行。 术语“单元测试”源自将这些组件视为不同“单元”的做法。 即使是最小的应用程序组件也会经历此测试过程。

在单元测试期间，每个组件都被单独评估以识别任何潜在问题。 但是，与外部实体（例如外部数据库、网络或文件）交互的组件不受单元测试的约束。 开发人员从开发的早期阶段就开始运行单元测试，以便在开发之前发现任何问题。

开发单元测试用例

单元测试涉及通过专用测试用例评估应用程序的每个部分。 每个测试用例对应一个特定的按钮按下或文本框条目，确保页面中所有元素的功能都经过全面测试。 为应用程序的每个页面创建多个单元测试用例，以确保不会遗漏任何元素。

由于有多种可用的单元测试框架，因此必须选择最适合开发人员编程语言的一种。 通过利用正确的框架，开发人员可以确保他们的单元测试有效且可靠。

功能测试与单元测试比较

功能测试

单元测试

它评估应用程序的可用性、可访问性和流程。

分别评估每个组件的操作。

包括多种测试方法，包括单元测试和系统测试。

它是功能测试的一个子集

需要更长的时间并检查应用程序的整体可用性。

它相对较短且效率更高，因为它专注于特定组件。

查找一般问题而不是具体问题。

它揭示了具体问题。

旨在验证软件的功能、可靠性和准确性，以确保它满足最终用户的需求。

旨在确保应用程序的各个单元或组件的功能，以在开发过程的早期发现缺陷并提高代码质量。

假设我们有一个允许用户预订拼车服务的移动应用程序。 为了测试应用程序的功能，我们可能会进行测试以确保用户可以完成以下任务：创建帐户登录应用程序搜索拼车选项选择行程并设置上车和下车地点查看预计价格和到达时间确认预订跟踪行程并接收实时更新评价行程并提供反馈

这些测试将模拟与应用程序的真实交互和场景，并确保应用程序按预期运行。

另一方面，单元测试将专注于应用程序的各个组件。 例如，我们可能会测试以下功能： 用户身份验证：用户是否可以创建帐户并登录？ 密码是否已加密并安全存储？搜索功能：应用程序是否会根据用户输入返回准确的搜索结果？ 搜索游乐设施时是否存在任何性能问题？预订流程：用户是否可以选择游乐设施并确认预订？ 预计价格和到达时间是否正确显示？乘车跟踪：该应用程序是否提供乘车位置和预计到达时间的实时更新？ 地图或 GPS 功能是否有任何问题？这些测试将有助于识别应用程序特定组件中的任何错误或问题，并确保每个单元都按预期工作。

通过进行功能测试和单元测试，我们可以确保移动应用程序正常运行并提供积极的用户体验。

对于一般功能测试，使用功能测试框架。

结合单元测试框架

功能测试和单元测试的目标是什么？

功能测试

功能测试旨在验证应用程序的可靠性、准确性和整体功能，以确保它满足最终用户的需求。 它涉及执行一系列测试，以复制真实场景和与应用程序的交互，以将结果与预期结果进行比较。 功能测试的主要目标是确保应用程序按预期运行并且所有组件都可以互操作。 功能测试对于识别可能对用户体验产生负面影响的任何问题至关重要。

单元测试

单元测试用于在开发过程的早期识别和修复缺陷。 单元测试的主要目标是提高代码质量并防止回归问题。 通过分别测试应用程序的每个组件，可以更轻松地确定问题的根本原因并快速解决问题。 此外，单元测试用作文档，清楚地概述了系统的用途

结论

了解单元测试和功能测试之间的区别对于测试人员和开发人员来说很重要。 虽然这两种测试方法都很关键，但它们有不同的用途，不应互换使用。 功能测试更为广泛和耗时，但它验证系统是否按照业务要求运行。

为了交付高质量的产品，在单元测试和功能测试之间取得适当的平衡至关重要，确保两种测试方法都得到适当执行。

原文:

Functional testing and unit testing are essential testing approaches in application development. In this blog post, we will provide an in-depth analysis of differences between the two types of testing, when to use each type, and the types of errors they identify. We’ll also look at how achieving a balance between the two testing approaches will result in the delivery of a high-quality product.

Functional Testing

Functional testing is a crucial testing methodology that concentrates on an application’s essential features to verify that it operates in accordance with business requirements. Prior to creating functional test cases, a thorough comprehension of the application’s operational needs is required.

Functional testing assesses an application’s page transitions, user interface, data storage, screen-switching capabilities, and overall performance, focusing on the user’s viewpoint. It ensures the application’s accessibility, usability, and overall user experience, making sure that the application meets the desired expectations of its intended users.

Developing Functional Test Cases

To conduct effective functional testing, a QA professional must thoroughly review the business requirements document, familiarize themselves with the application, and design a test strategy for the specific functionality that will be evaluated. They should also select the best tool for the functional testing required and compare actual results with expected results to determine the test’s success.

Unit testing

Unit testing allows for the individual testing of an application’s various components. Each component, including elements like text boxes, buttons, and text displays, undergoes scrutiny to ensure it performs as intended. The term “unit testing” derives from the practice of viewing these components as distinct “units”. Even the smallest app component undergoes this testing process.

During unit testing, each component is assessed separately to identify any potential issues. However, components that interface with external entities, such as external databases, networks, or files, are not subject to unit testing. Developers begin running unit tests from the early stages of development to catch any issues before they can progress.

Developing Unit Test Cases

Unit testing involves evaluating every part of an application through dedicated test cases. Each test case corresponds to a specific button press or text box entry, ensuring that the functionality of all elements in a page is thoroughly tested. Multiple unit test cases are created for each page of the application, to guarantee that no element is missed.

As there are several unit testing frameworks available, it’s essential to select the most suitable one for the developer’s programming language. By leveraging the right framework, developers can ensure that their unit tests are effective and reliable.

Functional Test vs. Unit Test Comparison

Functional Testing

Unit Testing

It evaluates the usability, accessibility, and flow of the application.

Evaluates the operation of each component separately.

Includes a variety of testing methods, including unit testing and system testing.

It’s a subset of functional testing

Takes longer and examines an application’s entire usability.

It is relatively shorter and more efficient as it concentrates on specific components.

Find generic problems without being specific.

It reveals specific problems.

Aims to verify the software’s functionality, dependability, and accuracy to ensure it satisfies end user needs.

Aims to ensure the functionality of individual units or components of an application to find defects early in the development process and enhance code quality.

Let’s say we have a mobile app that allows users to book a ride-sharing service. To test the app’s functionality, we might conduct tests to make sure that users can complete the following tasks: Create an accountLogin to the appSearch for ride-sharing optionsChoose a ride and set the pickup and drop-off locationsReview the estimated price and time of arrivalConfirm the bookingTrack the ride and receive real-time updatesRate the ride and provide feedback

These tests would mimic real-world interactions and scenarios with the application, and ensure that the app is working as intended.

Unit testing, on the other hand, would focus on individual components of the app. For example, we might test the following functions: User authentication: Can users create an account and log in? Are passwords encrypted and securely stored?Search functionality: Does the app return accurate search results based on user input? Are there any performance issues when searching for rides?Booking process: Are users able to select a ride and confirm the booking? Is the estimated price and time of arrival displayed correctly?Ride tracking: Does the app provide real-time updates on the ride’s location and ETA? Are there any issues with the map or GPS functionality?These tests would help identify any bugs or issues in specific components of the app, and ensure that each unit is working as intended.

By conducting both functional and unit tests, we can ensure that the mobile app is functioning correctly and providing a positive user experience.

For general functional testing, functional testing frameworks are employed.

Frameworks for unit testing are combined

What Do Functional Testing and Unit Testing Aim to Achieve?

Functional Testing

Functional testing aims to verify the applications dependability, accuracy, and overall functionality to ensure that it meets the end-users’ needs. It involves performing a series of tests that replicate real-world scenarios and interactions with the application to compare the results with the expected outcomes. The primary goal of functional testing is to ensure that the application operates as intended and that all components are interoperable. Functional testing is vital in identifying any issues that could negatively impact the user experience.

Unit Testing

Unit testing is used to identify and fix defects early in the development process. The primary objective of unit testing is to improve code quality and prevent regression issues. By testing each component of the application separately, it becomes easier to identify the root cause of problems and resolve them quickly. Additionally, unit tests serve as documentation, clearly outlining how the system is intended

Conclusion

It is important for testers and developers to understand the difference between unit testing and functional testing. While both testing methods are critical, they serve different purposes and should not be used interchangeably. Functional testing is more extensive and time-consuming, but it verifies that the system operates according to the business requirements.

To deliver a high-quality product, it is crucial to strike the right balance between unit testing and functional testing, ensuring that both testing methods are appropriately executed.

Items.Title: 您应该知道的 2023 年十大移动应用测试趋势 | Top 10 Mobile App Testing Trends for 2023 You Should Know
Items.Description:
年末是一个很好的机会来反思你的成就和下一年的计划。 移动应用程序开发团队可能会检查消费者行为、技术和市场的最新发展，然后他们可以考虑如何将它们纳入您的测试和开发策略。

为了帮助您规划 2023 年的移动应用在线测试策略，我们将在本文中讨论来年需要关注的移动测试趋势。

  1. 测试中的人工智能

几乎在开发周期的每个阶段，下一个进步都是人工智能 (AI)。 如果可以以与手动测试相同的质量水平完成任务，则应使用自动化。 预计人工智能的使用将很快增加。 通过结合人工智能和机器学习，稳定快速发布将变得更容易获得，这也将有助于解决问题和改进测试方法。 分析和报告也是有益的。

  1. 安全测试是重中之重

开发人员心目中最重要的问题之一是安全性，这可以通过进行移动应用程序测试来解决。 网络攻击继续影响着各行各业各种规模的公司，到 2023 年，预计将出现新的攻击形式。 您的公司处于危险之中，您客户的私人数据也是如此。 身份盗窃是可能的，连同登录密码和支付卡数据的泄露。

向远程工作人员的过渡和越来越多地使用物联网 (IoT) 设备使更多的接触成为可能。 有鉴于此，网络安全需要在每个增长水平上得到加强和考虑。

广告

  1. 质量作为营销工具

由于替代品种类繁多，用户对其移动应用程序体验抱有很高的期望。 由于这种需求，谷歌改变了一些东西，让更高质量的应用程序得以曝光。 另一方面，它可以隐藏或突出标记劣质产品。 您可能会通过 Android Vitals 获得额外的故障排除工具以及性能和稳定性监控。 移动应用程序的质量比以往任何时候都更加重要，因为买家会选择质量更高的商品。 全面的移动应用程序测试策略有助于确保部署高质量的应用程序。

  1. 无脚本和低代码测试自动化

低代码自动化测试使测试移动应用程序更容易、更快速。 非技术测试人员即使没有编程经验也可以构建和自动化测试用例。 团队中最了解目标受众的人可能会为开发人员提供有关潜在用户体验问题的有见地的信息。

通过将低代码自动化 Web 测试纳入您的方法，您可以扩大测试范围，同时简化和扩展公司的运营。

  1. 由于需要快速响应不断变化的市场 (MTTR)，因此缩短平均检测时间 (MTTD) 和平均解决时间是移动应用程序开发人员的首要任务。 利用 DevOps 研究和评估 (DORA)，指标是实现这一目标的一种方法。

    应用程序监控和错误报告系统可以轻松记录测量的指标，包括更改失败率、平均恢复时间、修改准备时间和部署频率。

    当 5G 网络变得更广泛时，移动应用程序开发人员将能够使用 5G 网络更快的上传和下载速率以及更低的延迟来设计更复杂和响应更快的应用程序。

    物联网已经主导了许多行业，从制造和运输到医疗保健和农业。 移动物联网应用程序的开发因此激增。 支持物联网的技术改变了业务运作的方式。 当然，智能手机应用程序只是物联网架构的一个组成部分。

    但是，当与最近的技术发展相结合时，它可能会提高效率、重组程序并从根本上改变您的生活方式或开展业务的方式。 与智能系统人类交互的主要界面是通过移动应用程序提供的。

    1. 无脚本测试自动化

    随着越来越多的企业尝试更好地利用其资源，无脚本自动化框架将在 2023 年变得越来越流行。 这些测试可以由没有编程或脚本专业知识的非高级开发人员创建，从而释放高端资源。

    由于大多数移动应用程序无法采用一刀切的策略，因此框架和定制将成为王道。

    1. 跨平台应用开发

    跨平台应用程序开发是 2023 年的主要趋势之一。这使您能够一次创建应用程序并在各种平台（例如 iOS 和 Android）上发布它。 跨平台开发可以节省时间和金钱，并保证您的软件在所有设备上的外观和感觉都相同。

    1. 移动应用程序质量作为营销工具

    由于市场上有如此多的移动应用程序，客户需要一流的体验。 为响应这一需求，Google Play 在 Play 商店中提供更多优质应用的曝光机会。 另一方面，它可能会停止销售劣质商品或警告客户。

    建议专注于技术卓越，Google Play 提供衡量标准的措施。 通过 Android Vitals，您可以关注性能和稳定性等参数，并利用新工具发现问题。

    最后说明

    应用程序开发和 QA 团队必须像激光一样专注于为消费者提供流畅和愉快的体验，同时兼顾当今移动优先世界对速度和安全性的高要求。 为了满足快速发生的需求和市场扩张，移动应用程序在线测试套件必不可少。 您可以通过包含全面的移动应用程序测试策略来确保制定合理的策略来扩展和发展您的公司。



原文:

The year’s ending is an excellent opportunity to reflect on your accomplishments and plan for the following year. The newest developments in consumer behaviors, technology, and the market may all be examined by mobile app development teams who can then consider how to incorporate them into your testing and development strategy.

To help you plan your mobile app testing online strategy for 2023, we’ll talk about the mobile testing trends to look out for in the upcoming year in this piece.

  1. Artificial Intelligence in Testing

The next advancement in almost every phase of the development cycle is artificial intelligence (AI). Automation should be used if a task can be completed with the same level of quality as a manual test. The use of AI is anticipated to increase soon. Stabilizing quick releases will be made more accessible by combining AI and machine learning, which will also aid in fixing issues and enhancing testing methods. Analytics and reporting are also beneficial.

  1. Security Testing is a Top Priority

One of the most significant issues on developers’ minds is security, which can be solved by conducting a mobile app test. Cyberattacks continue to affect companies of all sizes and across all industries, and in 2023, new forms of attacks are anticipated to emerge. Your company is in danger, but so is your customers’ private data. Identity theft is possible, along with compromising login passwords and payment card data.

Additional exposure is made possible by the transition to a remote workforce and the growing use of internet of things (IoT) devices. In light of this, cybersecurity needs to be enhanced and considered at every level of growth.

ADVERTISEMENT

  1. Quality As a Marketing Tool

Due to the wide variety of alternatives, users have high expectations for their mobile app experiences. Due to this demand, Google has changed things such that higher-quality apps get exposed. On the other hand, it can hide or prominently label inferior products. Google Play advises developers to place a priority on technical excellence and include details about the standards used to assess submissions.

You may get additional troubleshooting tools and performance and stability monitoring with Android Vitals. The quality of a mobile app is more crucial than ever since buyers will choose higher-quality items. A thorough mobile app testing strategy helps ensure the deployment of high-quality apps.

  1. Scriptless and Low-code Test Automation

Low-code automated testing makes testing mobile apps more accessible and faster. Non-technical testers may build and automate test cases even without programming experience. People on the team with the greatest first-hand knowledge of the target audience may provide developers with insightful information about potential UX issues.

By incorporating low-code automated web testing into your approach, you may expand the scope of your testing while simplifying and expanding your company’s operations.

  1. MTTD and MTTR As Critical KPIs

Reducing the mean time to detection (MTTD) and mean time to resolution is a top priority for mobile app developers due to the demand to respond swiftly to a changing market (MTTR). Utilizing DevOps Research and Assessment, or DORA, metrics is one approach to achieve this.

DORA measurements give teams a way to gauge performance and determine what needs to be improved. An app monitoring and error reporting system makes it simple to record the metrics measured, including change failure rate, mean time to recovery, lead time for modifications, and frequency of deployments.

Mobile app developers will be able to use 5G networks’ quicker upload and download rates and lower latency to design more sophisticated and responsive apps when 5G networks become more widely accessible.

The Internet of Things has dominated many industries, from manufacturing and transportation to healthcare and agriculture. Mobile IoT app development surged as a result of this. The way a business functions has been changed by IoT-enabled technologies. A smartphone app is, of course, just one component of the Internet of Things architecture.

But when paired with recent technology developments, it may increase efficiency, restructure procedures, and fundamentally alter how you live or conduct business. The main interface for interacting with intelligent systems humans is provided through mobile applications.

  1. Scriptless Test Automation

Scriptless automation frameworks will become more and more popular in 2023 as more businesses try to use their resources better. These tests may be created by non-senior developers without programming or scripting expertise, freeing up high-end resources.

Because most mobile apps won’t be able to employ a one-size-fits-all strategy, frameworks and customization will be king.

  1. Cross-Platform App Development

Cross-platform app development is one of the top trends for 2023. This enables you to create your app once and release it across various platforms, such as iOS and Android. Cross-platform development saves time and money and guarantees that your software will look and feel the same on all devices.

  1. Mobile App Quality as A Marketing Tool

Since there are so many mobile applications on the market, customers need a top-notch experience. In response to this demand, Google Play provides more exposure to higher-quality apps in the Play Store. On the other hand, it may stop selling inferior goods or warn customers about them.

Focusing on technological excellence is advised, and Google Play offers measures for measuring standards. Through Android vitals, you can keep an eye on parameters like performance and stability and utilize new tools to spot problems.

Final Note

The app development and QA teams must maintain a laser-like focus on giving consumers a smooth and happy experience while juggling the high demand for speed and security in today’s mobile-first world. To meet the demand and market expansion that is occurring quickly, a mobile app online testing suite is essential. You may ensure a sound strategy to expand and advance your firm by including a thorough mobile app testing strategy.
